title: Uncrewed Systems
description: Uncrewed Systems focuses on the design, operation, and integration of systems that function without onboard human presence, including drones, robotic platforms, and remote sensing systems. Students learn control systems, sensors, communication, embedded systems, and system integration to operate and manage uncrewed technologies. The field suits individuals who are analytical, tech-oriented, and interested in robotics, automation, and advanced engineering systems.

title: Uncrewed and Autonomous Systems Engineering
description: Uncrewed and Autonomous Systems Engineering focuses on designing, developing, and integrating autonomous technologies such as drones, robotic systems, and self-operating vehicles. Students learn control systems, robotics, artificial intelligence, embedded systems, sensors, and system integration to build intelligent systems that operate with minimal human intervention. The field suits individuals who are analytical, tech-oriented, and interested in robotics, automation, and advanced engineering systems.

title: Uncrewed Aircraft Systems
description: Uncrewed Aircraft Systems focuses on the design, operation, and management of drones and remotely piloted aircraft. Students learn aerodynamics, flight control systems, navigation, sensors, communication systems, and aviation regulations to develop and operate unmanned aerial platforms. The field suits individuals who are technically oriented, detail-focused, and interested in aviation, robotics, and autonomous flight technologies.

title: Uncrewed & Autonomous Systems
description: Uncrewed & Autonomous Systems focuses on the design, development, and operation of systems that function without direct human control, such as drones, autonomous vehicles, and robotic platforms. Students learn robotics, control systems, artificial intelligence, sensors, embedded systems, and navigation to build and manage autonomous technologies. The field suits individuals who are analytical, tech-oriented, and interested in robotics, automation, and intelligent systems.

title: Systems Engineering
description: Systems Engineering focuses on designing, integrating, and managing complex systems across their entire lifecycle. Students learn systems thinking, modeling, optimization, project management, and interdisciplinary integration to ensure all components of a system work effectively together. The field suits individuals who are analytical, detail-oriented, and interested in solving complex problems by coordinating technical and organizational elements.

title: Space Systems
description: Space Systems focuses on the design, integration, and operation of spacecraft, satellites, and related technologies. Students learn systems engineering, spacecraft design, control systems, embedded systems, and mission integration to develop and manage complex space technologies. The field suits individuals who are analytical, technically skilled, and interested in engineering systems for space exploration and satellite operations.

title: Space Physics
description: Space Physics focuses on studying physical processes in space, including solar activity, planetary magnetospheres, cosmic radiation, and interactions between the Sun and Earth. Students learn physics, electromagnetism, plasma physics, and data analysis to understand space environments and their impact on satellites and communication systems. The field suits individuals who are analytical, research-oriented, and interested in astrophysics, space science, and fundamental physical phenomena.

title: Space Operations
description: Space Operations focuses on the planning, control, and management of space missions, satellites, and ground systems. Students learn orbital mechanics, spacecraft systems, mission planning, space environment, and data analysis to operate and support space missions. The field suits individuals who are analytical, technically skilled, and interested in space technology, mission control, and aerospace operations.

title: Software Engineering
description: Software Engineering focuses on designing, developing, testing, and maintaining software systems using structured engineering principles. Students learn programming, software architecture, algorithms, databases, and system design to build reliable and scalable applications. The field suits individuals who are logical, detail-oriented, and interested in technology, problem-solving, and creating software solutions.

title: Simulation Science, Games and Animation
description: Simulation Science, Games and Animation focuses on designing interactive digital environments, simulations, and visual experiences using technology and creative storytelling. Students learn programming, computer graphics, game design, animation, physics simulation, and user interaction to develop games, virtual simulations, and immersive media. The field suits individuals who are creative, tech-oriented, and interested in combining design, storytelling, and technology.

title: Safety Management
description: Safety Management focuses on developing and implementing systems to prevent accidents, reduce risks, and ensure compliance with safety regulations across organizations. Students learn risk assessment, safety standards, incident investigation, environmental health, and management systems to create safe working environments. The field suits individuals who are detail-oriented, responsible, and interested in risk control, compliance, and workplace safety.

title: Technical Management
description: Technical Management focuses on leading and managing technology-driven projects, teams, and operations within organizations. Students learn project management, technology strategy, systems management, and leadership to bridge the gap between technical teams and business objectives. The field suits individuals who have a technical background and are interested in leadership, coordination, and decision-making in technology environments.

title: Pilot Operations
description: Pilot Operations focuses on the knowledge and skills required to operate aircraft safely and efficiently in commercial or private aviation. Students learn flight theory, navigation, meteorology, aviation safety, aircraft systems, and regulatory procedures, along with practical flight training. The field suits individuals who are disciplined, detail-oriented, and interested in aviation, technology, and high-responsibility operational roles.

title: Occupational Safety Management
description: Occupational Safety Management focuses on identifying, evaluating, and controlling workplace hazards to ensure employee health and safety. Students learn safety regulations, risk assessment, industrial hygiene, environmental health, and management systems to prevent accidents and ensure compliance. The field suits individuals who are detail-oriented, responsible, and interested in workplace safety, risk management, and regulatory standards.

title: Logistics and Supply Chain Management
description: Logistics and Supply Chain Management focuses on planning, coordinating, and optimizing the flow of goods, information, and resources from production to end consumers. Students learn logistics operations, procurement, inventory management, transportation, data analysis, and supply chain strategy to improve efficiency and reduce costs. The field suits individuals who are analytical, organized, and interested in operations, global trade, and problem-solving in dynamic business environments.

title: Leadership
description: Leadership focuses on developing the skills and strategies needed to guide individuals, teams, and organizations toward achieving goals. Students learn leadership theories, organizational behavior, communication, decision-making, and strategic management to effectively lead in diverse and dynamic environments. The field suits individuals who are proactive, people-oriented, and interested in management, influence, and organizational impact.

title: Human Security and Resilience
description: Human Security and Resilience focuses on protecting individuals and communities from threats such as conflict, disasters, poverty, and environmental risks while strengthening their ability to adapt and recover. Students learn human security frameworks, risk analysis, public policy, development studies, and crisis response to address global challenges and build sustainable, resilient societies. The field suits individuals who are socially driven, analytical, and interested in humanitarian work, policy, and global development.

title: Human Factors Psychology
description: Human Factors Psychology focuses on understanding how human cognition, perception, and behavior influence interactions with systems and environments. Students study cognitive psychology, human performance, decision-making, and ergonomics to improve usability, safety, and user experience. The field suits individuals who are analytical, user-focused, and interested in applying psychology to real-world design and performance challenges.

title: Human Factors
description: Human Factors focuses on understanding how people interact with systems, products, and environments to improve safety, efficiency, and user experience. Students learn cognitive psychology, ergonomics, human-computer interaction, and system design to optimize performance and reduce human error. The field suits individuals who are analytical, user-focused, and interested in designing systems that align with human capabilities and behavior.

title: Homeland Security and Intelligence
description: Homeland Security and Intelligence focuses on protecting national security through threat analysis, intelligence gathering, and crisis response. Students learn intelligence analysis, counterterrorism strategies, emergency management, public policy, and coordination across government agencies to prevent and respond to security threats. The field suits individuals who are analytical, disciplined, and interested in national security, intelligence operations, and public sector work.

title: Homeland Security
description: Homeland Security focuses on protecting a nation’s infrastructure, people, and institutions from threats such as terrorism, natural disasters, and cyber attacks. Students learn security strategy, emergency management, risk assessment, public policy, and coordination across government agencies to prevent, respond to, and recover from crises. The field suits individuals who are disciplined, analytical, and interested in public safety, national security, and crisis management.

title: Global Security & Intelligence Studies
description: Global Security & Intelligence Studies focuses on analyzing and managing security threats at national and international levels, including terrorism, cyber threats, and geopolitical risks. Students learn intelligence analysis, cybersecurity fundamentals, international relations, data analysis, and risk assessment to support decision-making in security and defense contexts. The field suits individuals who are analytical, discreet, and interested in global affairs, security strategy, and intelligence operations.

title: Global Conflict Studies
description: Global Conflict Studies examines the causes, dynamics, and resolution of conflicts at local, national, and international levels. Students study political systems, international relations, history, social structures, and conflict resolution strategies to understand issues such as war, terrorism, human rights, and peacebuilding. The field suits individuals who are analytical, globally minded, and interested in diplomacy, security, and social impact.

title: Global Business & Supply Chain Management
description: Global Business & Supply Chain Management focuses on managing international business operations and the flow of goods, information, and finances across global markets. Students learn supply chain strategy, logistics, procurement, international trade, operations management, and data analysis to optimize efficiency and competitiveness. The field suits individuals who are analytical, strategic, and interested in global commerce, coordination, and problem-solving in complex business environments.

title: Forensic Psychology
description: Forensic Psychology applies psychological principles to the criminal justice system, focusing on understanding criminal behavior, assessing offenders, and supporting legal processes. Students study psychology, criminology, mental health assessment, and legal procedures to evaluate individuals involved in crimes and court cases. The field suits those interested in human behavior, mental processes, and working within legal or correctional settings.

title: Forensic Biology
description: Forensic Biology focuses on the analysis of biological evidence in criminal investigations, particularly DNA, blood, bodily fluids, and other organic materials. Students learn molecular biology, genetics, biochemistry, and laboratory techniques used to identify individuals and reconstruct crime events. The field suits those who are detail-oriented, analytical, and interested in applying life sciences to real-world investigative work.

title: Forensic Accounting and Fraud Examination
description: Forensic Accounting and Fraud Examination focuses on investigating financial records to detect fraud, financial crimes, and legal disputes. Students learn accounting, auditing, financial analysis, fraud detection techniques, and legal procedures related to financial investigations. The field suits individuals who are detail-oriented, analytical, and interested in combining finance with investigation and compliance rather than general business operations.

title: Engineering Technology
description: Engineering Technology focuses on the practical application of engineering principles to support the implementation, operation, and maintenance of technical systems. Students learn applied engineering concepts, technical problem-solving, equipment operation, and hands-on skills related to manufacturing, electronics, and industrial systems. The field suits individuals who prefer practical, application-oriented work and want to work closely with technology and systems rather than theoretical design.

title: Engineering Physics
description: Engineering Physics focuses on applying fundamental physics principles to solve complex engineering problems, bridging theoretical science and practical technology development. Students study advanced physics, mathematics, materials science, and engineering concepts such as electronics, mechanics, and computational methods. The field suits individuals who are strong in math and physics and want flexible pathways into research, advanced engineering, or high-tech industries.

title: Engineering Management
description: Engineering Management focuses on applying management principles to engineering projects, teams, and organizations. Students learn project management, operations, finance, and leadership alongside engineering fundamentals to bridge technical and business functions. The field suits individuals with a technical background who want to move into leadership, coordination, or strategic roles rather than purely technical positions.

title: Engineering Fundamentals
description: Engineering Fundamentals focuses on foundational principles across multiple engineering disciplines, including basic physics, mathematics, materials, and introductory design concepts. Students learn problem-solving, technical analysis, and core engineering concepts that prepare them for specialized fields such as mechanical, electrical, or civil engineering. The field suits individuals who want a broad introduction to engineering before choosing a specific specialization.

title: Emergency Services
description: Emergency Services focuses on responding to urgent situations such as medical emergencies, accidents, and disasters through frontline operations and public safety support. Students learn emergency response procedures, basic medical care, crisis handling, and coordination with healthcare and public safety systems. The field suits individuals who are action-oriented, resilient, and interested in hands-on roles that involve protecting lives and providing immediate assistance in critical situations.

title: Emergency Management
description: Emergency Management focuses on preparing for, responding to, and recovering from natural and man-made disasters through coordinated planning and risk management. Students study disaster response, crisis management, public safety systems, risk assessment, and emergency planning. The field suits individuals who are organized, resilient, and interested in protecting communities and managing crises within government, nonprofit, or public safety sectors.

title: Electrical Engineering & Computer Science
description: Electrical Engineering & Computer Science focuses on the integration of hardware and software systems, combining electrical engineering principles with computing and software development. Students study circuits, electronics, signals, and embedded systems alongside programming, algorithms, and computer systems. The field suits individuals who are interested in both hardware and software and want flexibility to work across areas such as software engineering, AI, electronics, or system design.

title: Cyber Intelligence and Security
description: Cyber Intelligence and Security focuses on protecting digital systems, networks, and data through cybersecurity practices, threat analysis, and intelligence gathering. Students learn network security, cryptography, ethical hacking, digital forensics, and cyber threat intelligence. The field suits individuals who are analytical, detail-oriented, and interested in defending systems against cyber threats and understanding how attacks are detected and prevented.

title: Computational Mathematics
description: Computational Mathematics focuses on using mathematical models, algorithms, and numerical methods to solve complex problems through computing. Students study applied mathematics, programming, data analysis, numerical simulation, and algorithm design. The field suits individuals who are analytical, enjoy problem-solving, and are interested in applying mathematics in areas such as technology, data, engineering, or scientific computing.

title: Business Administration in Aviation
description: Business Administration in Aviation focuses on applying core business principles within the aviation industry, including airlines, airports, and aviation services. Students study finance, marketing, operations, and management alongside aviation-related topics such as airline operations and aviation regulations. The field suits individuals who are interested in business roles within aviation and prefer strategic or operational work over technical or engineering paths.

title: Business Administration in Aviation Management
description: Business Administration in Aviation Management focuses on applying business and management principles specifically within the aviation industry, including airlines, airports, and aviation services. Students study finance, operations management, strategy, and leadership alongside aviation-specific topics such as airline operations, airport management, and aviation regulations. The field suits individuals who are interested in managing aviation organizations and making strategic business decisions rather than technical or engineering roles.

title: Aviation Safety
description: Aviation Safety focuses on ensuring safe flight operations through risk management, accident prevention, and compliance with aviation regulations. Students learn safety systems, human factors, incident analysis, and aviation laws. The field suits individuals who are detail-oriented, responsible, and interested in maintaining safety standards and minimizing risks within aviation environments rather than technical design or business roles.

title: Aviation Maintenance Science
description: Aviation Maintenance Science focuses on the inspection, repair, and maintenance of aircraft to ensure safety and airworthiness. Students learn aircraft systems, maintenance procedures, diagnostics, aviation regulations, and safety standards. The field suits individuals who are hands-on, detail-oriented, and interested in the technical upkeep and reliability of aircraft rather than design or business roles.

title: Aviation Finance
description: Aviation Finance focuses on the financial management and economic aspects of the aviation industry, including airlines, airports, and aircraft leasing companies. Students study corporate finance, accounting, investment analysis, and aviation-specific topics such as airline revenue management, fleet financing, and cost control. The field suits individuals who are interested in finance and business decision-making within the aviation sector rather than technical or operational roles.

title: Aviation Business Administration
description: Aviation Business Administration focuses on the management and commercial operations of the aviation industry, including airlines, airports, and aviation service organizations. Students study business fundamentals such as finance, marketing, operations management, and aviation-specific topics like airline economics, airport management, and aviation regulations. The field suits individuals who are interested in business strategy and operations within the aviation sector rather than technical or engineering roles.

title: Aviation and Aerospace Sustainability
description: Aviation and Aerospace Sustainability focuses on reducing the environmental impact of air and space transportation through sustainable technologies, policies, and operational strategies. Students study climate science, sustainable aviation fuels, emissions reduction, environmental regulations, and the interaction between aerospace systems and the environment. The field suits individuals who are interested in sustainability, climate impact, and applying environmental solutions within the aviation and aerospace industry.

title: Aviation and Aerospace Safety
description: Aviation and Aerospace Safety focuses on ensuring the safety of aircraft, flight operations, and aerospace systems through risk management, accident prevention, and regulatory compliance. Students study aviation safety systems, human factors, accident investigation, safety regulations, and operational risk analysis. The field suits individuals who are detail-oriented, responsible, and interested in maintaining safety standards within aviation and aerospace environments rather than designing aircraft.

title: Aviation and Aerospace Safety
description: Aviation and Aerospace Safety focuses on ensuring the safety of aircraft, flight operations, and aerospace systems through risk management, accident prevention, and regulatory compliance. Students study aviation safety systems, human factors, accident investigation, safety regulations, and operational risk analysis. The field suits individuals who are detail-oriented, responsible, and interested in maintaining safety standards within aviation and aerospace environments rather than designing aircraft.

title: Aviation
description: Aviation focuses on the operation, management, and technical aspects of air transport systems, including flight operations, airport management, aviation safety, and air traffic control. Students learn about aviation systems, navigation, meteorology, regulations, and airline operations. The field suits individuals who are interested in the aviation industry, enjoy structured environments, and may pursue roles related to piloting, operations, or aviation management rather than purely engineering design.

title: Applied Meteorology
description: Applied Meteorology focuses on using atmospheric science to solve real-world problems related to weather, climate, and environmental systems. Students study meteorology, climatology, data analysis, forecasting models, and the interaction between the atmosphere and human activities. The field suits individuals who are analytical, interested in weather patterns and environmental impact, and want to apply scientific knowledge to areas such as forecasting, climate risk, and environmental planning.

title: Airworthiness Engineering
description: Airworthiness Engineering is a specialized discipline focused on ensuring that aircraft and their components meet rigorous safety and regulatory standards throughout their entire lifecycle. Students learn to evaluate structural integrity, system reliability, and environmental impacts while navigating complex certification processes from aviation authorities. The curriculum combines high-level engineering principles with legal frameworks to prevent mechanical failure and ensure public safety. This field is ideal for methodical thinkers who are dedicated to technical precision, safety auditing, and the continuous improvement of aviation standards.

title: Airport and Air Mobility Management
description: Airport and Air Mobility Management focuses on the complex coordination of airport infrastructure, traditional flight operations, and the integration of emerging Advanced Air Mobility (AAM) technologies. Students master terminal logistics, regulatory compliance, and airspace safety while exploring the development of vertiports and drone ecosystems. This field is ideal for strategic planners and operations-minded individuals who aim to optimize global aviation networks and lead the transition toward a highly automated and sustainable urban flight environment.

title: Airline Management
description: Airline Management focuses on the business and operational aspects of the aviation industry, including strategic planning, safety regulations, and fleet management. Students learn to optimize airline performance through revenue management, human resource coordination, and compliance with international aviation laws. This field is ideal for those who aim to lead complex organizational structures and ensure the seamless integration of technical flight operations with commercial success.

title: Air Traffic Management
description: This field focuses on the study, design, and operational management of aircraft and air traffic systems to ensure safe and efficient air transportation. Students gain a deep understanding of physics, mathematics, and materials science applied to aviation technology and global connectivity. It is designed for individuals interested in the technical and regulatory aspects of flight operations, defense, and space exploration.

title: Aerospace Physiology
description: Aerospace Physiology focuses on how the human body responds to extreme environments such as high altitude, low oxygen levels, acceleration forces, and space conditions. The program combines knowledge from physiology, medicine, and aviation to study issues like hypoxia, spatial disorientation, fatigue, and the physical limits of pilots and astronauts. Students learn how to maintain human performance and safety in aviation and space settings. This field is suitable for individuals interested in both healthcare and aviation, especially in human performance and safety.

title: Aerospace Business Analytics
description: Aerospace Business Analytics focuses on applying data analysis, business strategy, and analytics within the aviation and aerospace industry. The program combines business fundamentals with data-driven decision-making, covering areas such as aviation management, operations analytics, forecasting, supply chain, and financial analysis. Students learn how to analyze industry data, optimize airline or aerospace operations, and support strategic decisions using analytical tools. This field is suitable for individuals interested in both business and aviation, especially in data-focused roles.

title: Aeronautical Science - Flight
description: Aeronautical Science – Flight focuses on the principles of aviation along with hands-on pilot training. The program covers areas such as flight operations, aerodynamics, navigation, meteorology, aircraft systems, and aviation safety. Students receive both theoretical knowledge and practical flight experience, preparing them to operate aircraft and pursue professional pilot certifications. This field is suitable for individuals interested in aviation, flying, and careers in the airline or aerospace industry.

title: Interdisciplinary Writing
description: nterdisciplinary Writing focuses on developing advanced writing skills across multiple fields such as literature, media, communication, and professional contexts. The program integrates creative writing, academic writing, digital content creation, and rhetorical analysis. Students learn to adapt their writing style for different audiences and purposes while combining insights from various disciplines. This field is suitable for individuals interested in flexible, cross-industry writing careers that blend creativity with critical thinking and communication.

title: Literature
description: Literature focuses on the study of written works such as novels, poetry, drama, and essays across different time periods and cultures. The program explores literary analysis, critical theory, cultural context, and the evolution of language and storytelling. Students develop strong reading, writing, analytical, and interpretive skills while examining themes, narratives, and human experiences through texts. This field is suitable for individuals interested in writing, critical thinking, culture, and communication.

title: Sociology
description: Sociology focuses on the study of human society, social behavior, and the relationships between individuals, groups, and institutions. The program explores topics such as social inequality, culture, social change, family structures, education, and urbanization. Students develop skills in research methods, data analysis, and critical thinking to understand social patterns and address societal issues. This field is suitable for individuals interested in human behavior, social systems, and creating positive social impact.

title: Sociology
description: Sociology focuses on the study of human society, social behavior, and the relationships between individuals, groups, and institutions. The program explores topics such as social inequality, culture, social change, family structures, education, and urbanization. Students develop skills in research methods, data analysis, and critical thinking to understand social patterns and address societal issues. This field is suitable for individuals interested in human behavior, social systems, and creating positive social impact.

title: Political Science
description: Political Science focuses on the study of government systems, political behavior, public policies, and international relations. The program covers areas such as political theory, comparative politics, public administration, international politics, and policy analysis. Students develop skills in critical thinking, research, data analysis, and argumentation while understanding how political systems operate and influence society. This field is suitable for individuals interested in governance, law, public affairs, and global issues.

title: Physics
description: Physics focuses on the fundamental principles that govern matter, energy, motion, and the structure of the universe. The program covers areas such as mechanics, electromagnetism, thermodynamics, quantum physics, and relativity. Students develop strong analytical, mathematical, and problem-solving skills while learning to model and understand complex physical systems. This field is suitable for individuals interested in scientific research, technology development, and understanding how the universe works at both microscopic and cosmic levels.

title: Paralegal
description: Paralegal studies focus on supporting legal professionals by conducting research, preparing legal documents, and assisting in case management. The program covers areas such as legal research, contract law, litigation procedures, ethics, and legal writing. Students develop skills in analyzing legal information, organizing case files, and assisting attorneys in various legal processes. This field is suitable for individuals interested in law who prefer a supportive, research-oriented role rather than becoming a licensed attorney.

title: Pre-Nursing
description: Pre-Nursing is a preparatory program designed for students who plan to enter a Nursing program. It focuses on foundational subjects such as biology, chemistry, anatomy, physiology, and basic healthcare concepts. Students build the academic background and prerequisites needed for admission into nursing programs while developing essential skills in critical thinking and scientific understanding. This path is suitable for individuals who aim to pursue a career in nursing but have not yet entered a formal Nursing program.

title: Nursing (LPN to RN)
description: The Nursing (LPN to RN) bridge program is specifically designed for Licensed Practical Nurses (LPNs) seeking to advance their careers and become Registered Nurses (RNs). This intensive curriculum builds upon previous nursing experience, focusing on higher-level clinical reasoning, complex patient care management, and advanced leadership skills. Students transition from a task-oriented approach to a comprehensive, analytical role in nursing, encompassing in-depth studies of pathophysiology, pharmacology, and mental health. The program emphasizes the development of critical thinking necessary for autonomous nursing practice and prepares graduates for increased professional responsibilities and a wider scope of practice within the healthcare system.

title: Nursing (RN)
description: The Nursing (RN) program prepares registered nurses to provide high-quality patient care, support, and treatment in diverse healthcare settings. The curriculum integrates core health science knowledge such as anatomy, physiology, and pharmacology with rigorous clinical skills and professional ethics. Students are trained to perform comprehensive health assessments, develop detailed care plans, execute medical procedures, and collaborate closely with multidisciplinary medical teams to ensure patient safety and optimal recovery. This field is ideal for individuals with a strong sense of responsibility, the ability to work under pressure, and a profound commitment to improving community health and well-being.

title: Nuclear Medicine Technology
description: Nuclear Medicine Technology focuses on the use of radioactive materials and imaging technology to diagnose and treat diseases. The program covers topics such as radiopharmaceuticals, nuclear imaging techniques, human anatomy, radiation safety, and patient care. Students learn how to prepare and administer radioactive substances, operate imaging equipment, and produce diagnostic images for physicians. This field is suitable for individuals interested in advanced medical technology and working in clinical environments that support disease detection and treatment.

title: Music
description: Music focuses on the study and practice of musical performance, theory, composition, and production. The program covers areas such as music theory, history, performance techniques, composition, and audio technology while developing artistic expression and technical skills. Students learn how to perform, create, and analyze music across different styles and contexts. This field is suitable for individuals passionate about music, creativity, and pursuing careers in performance, education, or the music industry.

title: Networking & Cybersecurity
description: Networking & Cybersecurity focuses on building, managing, and securing computer networks and information systems. The program covers areas such as network infrastructure, cybersecurity principles, ethical hacking, encryption, cloud security, and risk management. Students learn how to protect systems from cyber threats, monitor network activity, and ensure data integrity and privacy across digital environments. This field is suitable for individuals interested in IT systems, cybersecurity, and safeguarding digital assets in an increasingly connected world.

title: Networking & Data Security
description: Networking & Data Security focuses on the design, management, and protection of computer networks and digital information systems. The program covers topics such as network architecture, cybersecurity principles, data protection, encryption, cloud computing, and system administration. Students learn how to build secure networks, detect and prevent cyber threats, and ensure the integrity and confidentiality of data. This field is suitable for individuals interested in IT systems, cybersecurity, and protecting digital infrastructure in organizations.

title: Radiologic Science
description: Radiologic Science focuses on the use of medical imaging technologies to diagnose and monitor diseases. The program covers areas such as radiography, imaging techniques, anatomy, patient care, radiation safety, and medical imaging equipment. Students learn how to operate imaging systems like X-ray, CT, and MRI, ensure patient safety, and produce accurate diagnostic images for clinical evaluation. This field is suitable for individuals interested in healthcare technology and working in clinical environments that support medical diagnosis and treatment.

title: Environmental Sustainability
description: Environmental Sustainability focuses on developing strategies to protect natural resources and promote long-term environmental balance. The program covers topics such as sustainability principles, environmental policy, resource management, renewable energy, climate change, and sustainable development. Students learn how to assess environmental impacts, design sustainable solutions, and implement practices that reduce ecological footprints across industries and communities. This field is suitable for individuals interested in sustainability, environmental protection, and creating solutions for global environmental challenges.

title: Materials and Manufacturing Engineering
description: Materials and Manufacturing Engineering focuses on the design, development, and production of materials and the processes used to manufacture products. The program covers topics such as materials science, metallurgy, polymers, ceramics, manufacturing processes, and quality control while developing skills in analyzing material properties and optimizing production systems. Students learn how to select appropriate materials, improve product performance, and design efficient manufacturing processes across industries. This field is suitable for individuals interested in materials innovation, industrial production, and engineering solutions in sectors such as manufacturing, aerospace, automotive, and electronics.

title: Civil and Mechanical Engineering
description: Civil and Mechanical Engineering focuses on the design, construction, and maintenance of infrastructure and mechanical systems. The program combines principles from both civil and mechanical engineering, covering areas such as structural design, fluid mechanics, thermodynamics, materials science, and mechanical systems. Students learn how to design buildings, transportation systems, and machines while applying engineering principles to solve real-world problems. This field is suitable for individuals interested in designing physical systems, working on infrastructure projects, and developing mechanical technologies across industries such as construction, manufacturing, and energy.

title: Bioengineers and Chemical Engineers
description: Bioengineers and Chemical Engineers focus on applying principles of engineering, chemistry, and biology to design and optimize processes, products, and systems in industries such as pharmaceuticals, biotechnology, energy, and materials. The program covers topics such as chemical processes, thermodynamics, transport phenomena, bioprocess engineering, and materials science while developing strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Students learn how to design production systems, improve efficiency, and develop innovative solutions that integrate biological and chemical technologies. This field is suitable for individuals interested in engineering applications of science, industrial processes, and developing technologies in healthcare, energy, and manufacturing sectors.

title: Emergency Medical & Health Services
description: Emergency Medical and Health Services focuses on providing immediate medical care and support in emergency and pre-hospital settings. The program covers areas such as emergency response, trauma care, patient assessment, medical procedures, and healthcare systems while developing skills in critical decision-making and rapid intervention. Students learn how to stabilize patients, respond to medical emergencies, and coordinate with healthcare teams in high-pressure environments. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in fast-paced healthcare settings and providing urgent medical assistance to save lives.

title: Elementary Education
description: Elementary Education focuses on preparing individuals to teach and support the academic and social development of children at the primary school level. The program covers key subjects such as literacy, mathematics, science, and social studies alongside teaching methods, classroom management, child development, and educational psychology. Students learn how to design lesson plans, assess student progress, and create inclusive learning environments that support diverse learners. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about working with children and contributing to early education and foundational learning.

title: Earth Sciences
description: Earth Sciences focuses on the study of the Earth’s physical structure, natural processes, and environmental systems. The program explores areas such as geology, geophysics, geochemistry, oceanography, and environmental science while examining how natural forces shape the planet. Students learn how to analyze rocks, minerals, landforms, and environmental data to understand processes such as plate tectonics, earthquakes, climate systems, and natural resource formation. This field is suitable for individuals interested in understanding Earth systems, environmental change, and the scientific processes that influence the planet’s development and sustainability.

title: Atmospheric Science
description: Atmospheric Science focuses on the study of the Earth's atmosphere, weather systems, and climate processes. The program explores topics such as meteorology, climate dynamics, atmospheric chemistry, weather forecasting, and environmental monitoring. Students learn how atmospheric processes influence weather patterns, climate change, and environmental conditions using scientific analysis, mathematical models, and data interpretation. This field is suitable for individuals interested in weather prediction, climate research, and understanding how atmospheric systems affect the environment and human activities.

title: Computer and Electrical Engineering
description: Computer and Electrical Engineering focuses on the design, development, and integration of electrical systems, electronic devices, and computer technologies. The program combines principles from electrical engineering and computer engineering, covering areas such as circuits, digital systems, embedded systems, computer architecture, signal processing, and communication networks. Students develop strong skills in hardware and software integration while learning how electronic systems interact with computing technologies. This field is suitable for individuals interested in building advanced electronic systems, developing computing hardware, and working at the intersection of electronics, computing, and information technology.

title: Community Health
description: Community Health focuses on improving health outcomes and well-being within communities through education, prevention, and health promotion initiatives. The program covers topics such as public health principles, epidemiology, health education, community health assessment, and disease prevention strategies. Students learn how to design and implement health programs, analyze community health needs, and promote healthy behaviors across diverse populations. This field is suitable for individuals interested in public health, community outreach, and developing programs that improve health awareness and quality of life at the population level.

title: Communication Studies
description: Communication Studies focuses on how people create, share, and interpret messages across interpersonal, organizational, media, and public contexts. The program explores communication theory, media studies, public speaking, interpersonal communication, and digital communication while helping students develop strong analytical and presentation skills. Students learn how communication influences relationships, organizations, culture, and public opinion. This field is suitable for individuals interested in media, public speaking, social interaction, and understanding how communication shapes modern society and professional environments.

title: Applied Management
description: Applied Management focuses on developing practical leadership and organizational skills used to manage teams, projects, and business operations. The program combines core business knowledge with real-world management practices, covering topics such as organizational behavior, project management, operations management, communication, and strategic decision-making. Students learn how to apply management principles in workplace settings, improve organizational performance, and lead teams effectively. This field is suitable for individuals who want to advance into supervisory or managerial roles across industries such as business services, retail, logistics, and corporate operations.

title: Physics and Astronomy
description: Physics and Astronomy focuses on understanding the fundamental laws governing matter, energy, space, and the universe. The program combines studies in classical and modern physics with astronomy, covering topics such as mechanics, electromagnetism, thermodynamics, quantum physics, astrophysics, and cosmology. Students develop strong analytical, mathematical, and research skills while learning how physical principles explain natural phenomena on Earth and in space. This field is suitable for individuals interested in scientific discovery, mathematical problem-solving, and exploring the physical universe through research, technology, and scientific analysis.

title: MTTM (Master of Tourism & Travel Management)
description: Specialized professional degree focusing on the global travel industry, hospitality management, sustainable tourism, and aviation.

title: M.A. English / Malayalam
description: Advanced study of literature, linguistics, and philology. Focuses on literary criticism, translation studies, and the cultural history of the language.

title: M.Sc. Physics / Chemistry / Mathematics
description: Deep dive into specialized research areas like Quantum Mechanics, Organic Synthesis, or Abstract Algebra. Focuses on preparing students for CSIR-NET/JRF and doctoral research.

title: M.Com (Finance & Management)
description: An advanced study of corporate finance, investment management, and strategic business leadership. It is designed for students aiming for senior financial advisory or management roles.

title: Computer Science
description: Computer Science focuses on the study of computational systems, algorithms, and software development used to process and manage information. The program covers topics such as programming, data structures, algorithms, computer systems, databases, artificial intelligence, and software engineering. Students learn how to design, develop, and optimize software applications and computing systems while solving complex technical problems. This field is suitable for individuals interested in technology, logical problem-solving, and building software or computing solutions for industries such as technology, finance, healthcare, and research.

title: B.Sc. Botany / Zoology / Biotechnology
description: Life sciences programs covering plant biology, animal physiology, and the application of biological systems in technology and medicine.

title: Mathematics / Physics / Chemistry
description: Core pure science programs that delve into advanced theories and laboratory research. These degrees provide the foundation for careers in R&D, academia, and engineering-adjacent fields.

title: B.A. Economics
description: Focuses on micro and macroeconomics, econometrics, and financial markets. It trains students to analyze data and understand global economic policies and consumer behavior.

title: B.A. Economics
description: Focuses on micro and macroeconomics, econometrics, and financial markets. It trains students to analyze data and understand global economic policies and consumer behavior.

title: B.A. Journalism & Mass Communication
description: A dynamic program focusing on news reporting, digital media, public relations, and advertising. It covers the ethics of journalism, media laws, film studies, and broadcast production.

title: B.A. English Language & Literature
description: This program offers an immersive exploration of the English language through its historical evolution and its vast body of global literature. Students delve into various genres, including poetry, drama, fiction, and non-fiction, spanning from the Medieval and Renaissance periods to Post-colonial and Contemporary works. Beyond reading, the curriculum emphasizes Literary Theory, Linguistics, and Cultural Studies, training students to analyze texts through diverse lenses such as feminism, structuralism, and historicism. The degree is highly interdisciplinary, often connecting literary works with their social, political, and psychological contexts. It is ideal for students with a passion for storytelling, critical inquiry, and creative expression.

title: Accounts & Audit / Finance
description: This specialization offers a comprehensive study of financial accounting, corporate auditing, and strategic financial management. The program is designed to bridge the gap between academic theory and professional practice, covering essential areas such as Taxation (GST and Income Tax), Cost Accounting, and Financial Reporting Standards. Students gain a deep understanding of how to analyze financial statements, manage corporate portfolios, and ensure regulatory compliance. This track is highly recommended for students aiming for professional certifications like CA, ACCA, CFA, or CMA, as well as roles in investment banking and corporate finance.

title: Real Estate
description:
Real Estate focuses on property investment, development, valuation, and real estate finance. The field combines finance, economics, urban development, and property law to understand how land and buildings are bought, sold, managed, and developed.
Students study real estate investment analysis, property valuation, real estate finance, urban economics, real estate law, development planning, and market analysis.
This major is suitable for students interested in property markets, investment strategy, urban development, and asset management.

title: Law, Politics and Economics
description: Law, Politics, and Economics is an interdisciplinary major that examines the complex interactions between legal systems, political structures, and economic theories. It explores how legal frameworks shape economic behavior, how political power influences law-making, and how economic conditions impact social stability and governance. Students analyze public policy, international relations, and market regulations, gaining a holistic understanding of how these three pillars jointly govern modern societies and global markets.

title: Business Analytics
description:
Business Analytics focuses on using data, statistical methods, and technology to support business decision-making. The field combines business knowledge with data analysis, predictive modeling, data visualization, and programming tools (e.g., SQL, Python, BI platforms).
Students learn how to transform raw data into actionable insights that improve strategy, operations, marketing performance, financial planning, and risk management.

title: Business Studies
description: Business Studies is a broad business discipline focused on understanding how organizations operate, compete, and grow in domestic and global markets. The field integrates management, marketing, finance, economics, entrepreneurship, operations, and strategic planning to develop analytical, leadership, and decision-making skills applicable across industries.

title: International Business Management
description: International Business Management prepares students to manage and lead organizations with global operations. It encompasses the study of international trade, cross-cultural communication, global strategy, and international finance. Students learn how to navigate diverse political, legal, and economic systems to solve complex business challenges, negotiate across cultures, and oversee multinational teams and supply chains effectively.

title: International Logistics Management
description: International Logistics Management focuses on planning, coordinating, and optimizing the movement of goods, information, and resources across international supply chains, including transportation, warehousing, customs, inventory management, and global distribution systems.

title: Global Finance
description: Global Finance focuses on financial systems, markets, and decision-making in an international context, including cross-border capital flows, global financial markets, corporate finance, investment analysis, and risk management across multinational environments.

title: Global Entrepreneurship
description: Global Entrepreneurship focuses on the creation, management, and growth of business ventures across international borders. It combines core entrepreneurship skills with a deep understanding of global economics, cross-cultural management, and international trade regulations. Students learn to identify global market opportunities, navigate diverse business environments, and develop strategies for scaling innovations in foreign markets

title: Retail and E-Business Entrepreneurship
description: This major integrates traditional retail management with modern e-commerce strategies and entrepreneurial skills. It focuses on the evolving landscape of consumer behavior, omni-channel retailing, digital marketing, supply chain management, and data analytics. Students learn how to launch new ventures, manage online and offline store operations, and develop strategies to drive sales and customer engagement in a digital-first economy.

title: Applied Business Data Science
description: This field focuses on applying data science techniques to solve business problems and support strategic decision-making. Students learn data analysis, statistics, machine learning, data management, and visualization alongside business domains such as finance, marketing, and operations. It is suitable for individuals interested in working with data in business contexts, translating complex datasets into actionable insights, and supporting data-driven strategies in organizations.

title: Management
description: This field focuses on planning, organizing, and leading organizations to achieve strategic and operational goals. Students learn core areas such as leadership, organizational behavior, operations, finance, marketing, and decision-making, along with problem-solving and communication skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in managing teams, improving business performance, and taking on leadership roles across various industries and organizational settings.

title: Sustainability Management
description: This field focuses on managing organizations and operations with an emphasis on environmental sustainability and long-term impact. Students learn sustainability strategy, environmental policy, resource management, corporate social responsibility, and sustainable business practices, along with analytical and decision-making skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in integrating environmental considerations into business strategy, improving organizational sustainability performance, and working in roles that balance economic, environmental, and social goals.

title: Strategic Sports Management
description: Strategic Sports Management focuses on the strategic planning and management of sports organizations, events, and brands, integrating business strategy, marketing, governance, and leadership within the global sports industry.

title: International Logistics & Supply Chain Management
description: This field focuses on managing the global flow of goods, services, and information across supply chains. Students learn logistics operations, procurement, transportation, inventory management, international trade, and supply chain strategy, along with analytical and problem-solving skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in optimizing operations, coordinating across global networks, and improving efficiency in industries such as manufacturing, retail, and e-commerce.

title: Luxury, Sales and Fashion Management
description: This field focuses on managing luxury brands, sales strategies, and fashion business operations within global markets. Students learn luxury brand management, retail strategy, consumer behavior, sales management, and fashion industry dynamics, along with marketing and business development skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in working in high-end fashion and luxury sectors, combining creativity with business strategy and customer experience management.

title: Strategic Marketing Management
description: This field focuses on developing long-term marketing strategies that align with overall business objectives in competitive markets. Students learn strategic planning, brand management, market analysis, consumer insights, and integrated marketing communications, along with data-driven decision-making and leadership skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in high-level marketing roles, managing brand growth, and designing strategies that drive sustainable business performance.

title: Digital Marketing Management
description: This field focuses on managing digital marketing strategies and teams to drive brand growth and business performance through online channels. Students learn digital strategy, performance marketing, data analytics, content management, customer engagement, and platform optimization, along with leadership and decision-making skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in overseeing digital campaigns, managing marketing operations, and using data to guide strategic marketing decisions in fast-paced environments.

title: Psychology & Management
description: This field combines psychological principles with management practices to understand human behavior in organizational settings. Students learn topics such as organizational psychology, leadership, motivation, decision-making, and human resource management alongside core business concepts. It is suitable for individuals interested in managing people, improving workplace performance, and applying behavioral insights to leadership and organizational development.

title: Business Intelligence & Data Science
description: This field focuses on using data to support business decision-making by combining data science techniques with business intelligence tools and strategic thinking. Students learn data analysis, statistics, machine learning, data visualization, and database management alongside business knowledge such as operations, marketing, and finance. It is suitable for individuals interested in working with data, solving business problems, and translating complex datasets into actionable insights in corporate environments.

title: International Business
description: This field focuses on how businesses operate across global markets, integrating knowledge of international trade, finance, marketing, and cross-cultural management. Students learn about global business strategy, international economics, supply chains, and market entry strategies while developing analytical and communication skills for multicultural environments. It is suitable for individuals interested in working with multinational companies, global trade, and roles that require adaptability and strategic thinking in an international context.

title: Applied Data Science & Business Analytics
description: This field focuses on using data analysis, statistical methods, and technology to support business decision-making. Students learn data analytics, programming, statistics, machine learning basics, and business intelligence, along with how to interpret and communicate insights for strategic use. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy working with data, combining technical and business skills, and solving real-world problems through analysis.

title: Finance & Management
description: This field focuses on how organizations operate, make financial decisions, and manage people, resources, and strategies in a global business environment. Students learn core areas such as finance, accounting, marketing, operations, and organizational behavior, along with analytical and decision-making skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in leadership, business strategy, financial planning, and working in dynamic corporate or entrepreneurial settings.

title: Finance
description: This field focuses on managing money, investments, and financial systems for individuals and organizations. Students learn financial analysis, investment strategies, accounting basics, taxation, risk management, and economic principles. It is suitable for individuals who are interested in numbers, decision-making, and understanding how financial markets and businesses operate.

title: Allied Health
description: Allied Health focuses on supporting healthcare systems through diagnostic, therapeutic, and clinical support services that assist physicians and other healthcare professionals. The program provides foundational knowledge in human anatomy, physiology, medical terminology, healthcare systems, and patient care practices while developing practical skills used in clinical and laboratory environments. Students learn how to contribute to patient care, healthcare operations, and medical support services in hospitals, clinics, and community health organizations. This field is suitable for individuals interested in healthcare who want to work in medical support, clinical assistance, diagnostics, or health service delivery.

title: Associate in Applied Science in Accounting
description: Associate in Applied Science in Accounting is a specialization designed to prepare students for practical accounting careers by teaching essential skills such as bookkeeping, payroll, taxation, and financial reporting. It focuses on hands-on training for entry-level roles in accounting departments, small businesses, or financial offices.

title: Banking & Finance
description: Banking & Finance focuses on the management of money, financial institutions, and financial markets, including areas such as investment, corporate finance, banking operations, risk management, and financial analysis. Students learn financial principles, economic concepts, data analysis, and decision-making skills to evaluate investments and manage financial resources effectively. This field suits individuals who are analytical, detail-oriented, and interested in financial systems, markets, and business decision-making.

title: International Trade and Business
description: This field focuses on the exchange of goods and services across international markets, combining business management with global trade and economic principles. Students learn international trade theory, global business strategy, import and export operations, trade regulations, and cross-border finance while developing analytical and decision-making skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in global commerce, multinational business operations, and careers involving international markets and trade systems.

title: Theological Studies
description: Theological Studies is an academic discipline that involves the systematic study of religious beliefs, divine nature, and the relationship between the sacred and the human experience. It explores the historical development of religious doctrines, sacred texts, and ethical frameworks through critical analysis and philosophical inquiry. The field integrates history, linguistics, and sociology to understand how spiritual traditions shape culture, morality, and social structures.

title: Electrical Engineering Technology
description: This field focuses on the practical application of electrical engineering principles to install, operate, test, and maintain electrical and electronic systems. Students learn circuits, power systems, electronics, control systems, and troubleshooting techniques, with strong emphasis on hands-on technical skills. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy working with electrical systems, solving technical problems, and applying engineering knowledge in real-world industrial and infrastructure environments.

title: Esports Management Concentration
description: Esports Management Concentration focuses on the business, operations, and strategic management of the esports and competitive gaming industry. Students learn how esports organizations, tournaments, teams, and gaming events are managed while developing skills in marketing, event management, sponsorship, digital media, and sports business operations. The program combines business principles with knowledge of the rapidly growing gaming and entertainment ecosystem. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in gaming culture and want to work in management, marketing, or operations roles within esports organizations and the broader digital entertainment industry.

title: Human Performance
description: Human Performance is the scientific study of how to optimize human capabilities, focusing on the intersection of physiology, biomechanics, and psychology. It involves analyzing movement, metabolic processes, and cognitive factors to enhance athletic performance, workplace productivity, and overall quality of life. Professionals in this field design evidence-based training programs, assess physical resilience, and develop strategies for recovery and injury prevention, making it a critical discipline for high-stakes environments such as professional sports, military operations, and corporate wellness.

title: Electrical Construction and Maintenance Electrician
description: This field focuses on the installation, maintenance, and repair of electrical systems in residential, commercial, and industrial settings. Students learn electrical circuits, wiring systems, power distribution, safety codes, and troubleshooting techniques, along with hands-on training. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy practical work, problem-solving, and working with electrical systems in construction and maintenance environments.

title: Communication Design
description: This field focuses on visual communication through design, combining creativity with strategic messaging to convey ideas effectively across different media. Students learn graphic design, visual storytelling, branding, typography, digital media, and user-centered design while developing creative and technical skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in design, media, and creating visual content for communication in industries such as advertising, branding, and digital platforms.

title: Music Vocal (BS)
description: Music Vocal focuses on developing professional vocal performance skills while building a strong foundation in music theory, ear training, and music history. The program trains students in vocal technique, breath control, diction, stage performance, and interpretation across different musical styles such as classical, contemporary, and musical theatre. Students also gain experience through solo performances, ensembles, and recitals while learning collaboration and performance preparation. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about singing and want to pursue careers in vocal performance, recording, teaching, or the broader music and entertainment industry.

title: Music Education – Choral (BM)
description: Music Education – Choral focuses on preparing students to teach vocal and choral music in educational and community settings. The program combines training in music theory, vocal performance, conducting, music pedagogy, and choral literature while developing skills in arranging, rehearsal techniques, and ensemble leadership. Students also study educational psychology and teaching methods to effectively guide singers of different ages and skill levels. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about vocal music, enjoy working with groups, and want to pursue careers in music education, choir direction, or community music leadership.

title: STEM Master of Business Administration
description: STEM Master of Business Administration combines traditional business management education with strong analytical and technology-focused training. Students develop skills in strategic management, finance, operations, and leadership while also learning data analytics, quantitative decision-making, and technology-driven business strategies. The program prepares graduates to lead organizations in data-driven and innovation-oriented environments. This field is suitable for individuals who want to build advanced business leadership skills while working with technology, analytics, and modern business systems.

title: Journalism Concentration
description: Journalism Concentration focuses on gathering, verifying, and presenting news and information to the public through various media platforms. Students learn how to research stories, conduct interviews, analyze information, and produce content for print, digital, broadcast, and multimedia journalism. The program typically covers news writing, media ethics, reporting techniques, digital storytelling, and media law. This field is suitable for individuals who are curious, analytical, and interested in communicating important events and issues to the public through credible and engaging storytelling.

title: Technical Communication and Emergent Media
description: This field focuses on creating and managing technical and digital content across emerging media platforms. Students learn technical writing, digital communication, content design, user experience, and the use of modern media tools and technologies. It is suitable for individuals who are interested in both communication and technology, and want to translate complex technical information into clear, engaging content for diverse audiences.

title: Anatomy and Cell Biolo
description: Anatomy and Cell Biology focuses on the structure, organization, and function of cells, tissues, and organ systems in the human body and other organisms. The program combines studies in cell biology, molecular biology, histology, physiology, and biomedical research methods to understand how cells develop, interact, and contribute to health and disease. Students develop strong laboratory and analytical skills through experimental research, microscopy, and molecular techniques. This field is suitable for individuals interested in biomedical research, human biology, and advancing scientific knowledge related to cellular function, disease mechanisms, and medical science.

title: Master of Divinity in Christian Ministries
description: Master of Divinity in Christian Ministries focuses on preparing students for leadership and pastoral roles within Christian communities and religious organizations. The program combines advanced studies in Christian theology, biblical studies, pastoral counseling, ethics, and ministry leadership with training in preaching, worship planning, and community outreach. Students develop the skills to guide congregations, provide spiritual counseling, and organize ministry programs that support faith development and community engagement. This field is suitable for individuals called to Christian leadership who want to serve as pastors, ministry leaders, or spiritual counselors in churches and faith-based organizations.

title: Nuclear Medical Applications
description: Nuclear Medical Applications focuses on the use of radioactive materials and nuclear technologies in medical diagnosis and treatment. Students study radiation physics, nuclear medicine imaging, radiopharmaceuticals, and radiation safety while learning how nuclear techniques are used to detect and treat diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular conditions. The program combines medical science with nuclear technology to support advanced diagnostic imaging and therapeutic procedures in healthcare. This field is suitable for individuals interested in medical technology, healthcare innovation, and applying nuclear science to improve patient diagnosis and treatment.

title: Applied Behavior Analysis
description: Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) focuses on understanding and improving human behavior through evidence-based behavioral principles. The field applies systematic observation, assessment, and intervention strategies to increase positive behaviors and reduce challenging behaviors. ABA is widely used in clinical, educational, and therapeutic settings, particularly in supporting individuals with developmental disorders, including autism spectrum disorder. It is suitable for individuals interested in behavioral science, intervention design, and measurable outcomes in human development.

title: Imaging Science
description: This field focuses on the use of imaging technologies to visualize and analyze the human body for diagnosis and medical evaluation. Students learn medical imaging techniques such as X-ray, MRI, and CT, along with anatomy, image analysis, and patient care. It is suitable for individuals interested in healthcare technology, who are detail-oriented, and want to work with advanced diagnostic equipment in clinical or laboratory settings.

title: Professional Sales Concentration
description: Professional Sales Concentration focuses on developing the skills and strategies required to sell products and services in competitive business environments. Students learn how to build client relationships, identify customer needs, negotiate deals, and manage sales pipelines. The curriculum typically covers sales management, consumer behavior, marketing strategy, communication, and data-driven sales techniques. This field is suitable for individuals who are persuasive, confident communicators and interested in working directly with clients to drive revenue and business growth.

title: Law
description: Law focuses on the study of legal systems, principles, and regulations that govern societies, including areas such as civil law, criminal law, corporate law, and international law. Students develop skills in legal analysis, critical thinking, research, and argumentation while learning how laws are created, interpreted, and applied. This field suits individuals who are analytical, detail-oriented, and interested in justice, governance, and solving complex legal issues across various sectors.

title: Graphic & Media Design
description: This field focuses on visual communication through graphic design and digital media. Students learn typography, layout, branding, visual storytelling, and design software, along with principles of user experience and media production. It is suitable for individuals who are creative, have a strong visual sense, and want to create designs for digital platforms, advertising, and media industries.

title: Church Multiplication
description: This major focuses on theological studies, church leadership, and strategies for expanding faith-based communities. Students develop knowledge in religious doctrine, pastoral leadership, organizational development, evangelism, and community engagement within religious contexts. The program is suitable for students pursuing roles in ministry leadership, church administration, and faith-based organizational development.

title: Bible and Theology
description: This major focuses on the study of biblical texts, theological doctrines, and religious thought within historical and contemporary contexts. Students develop knowledge in scriptural analysis, systematic theology, ethics, church history, and religious leadership. The program is suitable for students interested in ministry, religious education, theological research, and faith-based organizational leadership.

title: Biomedical Sciences
description: Biomedical Sciences is a multidisciplinary field that combines biology and medicine to focus on the health of both humans and animals. It involves the study of how the body works, how diseases and conditions occur, and how they can be treated with a focus on laboratory-based research and clinical applications. Students explore areas such as genetics, microbiology, pharmacology, and immunology to understand the molecular and cellular mechanisms of life. This major is fundamental for developing new medical treatments, vaccines, and diagnostic tools, serving as a critical bridge between basic science and clinical practice.

title: Athletic Training
description: Athletic Training is an allied health care profession that encompasses the prevention, examination, diagnosis, treatment, and rehabilitation of emergent, acute, or chronic injuries and medical conditions. It integrates clinical education with the study of anatomy, physiology, and sports medicine to provide comprehensive care for physically active individuals. Athletic trainers work closely with physicians to optimize patient activity and participation in athletics, work, and life, making it an essential discipline for high-performance sports environments and clinical settings.

title: Performance
description: Performance focuses on developing advanced musical skills for professional stage, recording, and ensemble settings. The program provides intensive training in instrumental or vocal technique, musical interpretation, stage presence, and repertoire across different musical styles. Students study music theory, ear training, music history, and ensemble collaboration while gaining practical experience through recitals, concerts, and live performances. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about performing music and want to pursue careers in professional performance, recording, teaching, or the broader music industry.

title: Mechanical Engineering Technology
description: This field focuses on the practical application of mechanical engineering principles to design, operate, and maintain machinery and mechanical systems. Students learn topics such as mechanics, thermodynamics, manufacturing processes, and system maintenance, with a strong emphasis on hands-on skills and technical problem-solving. It is suitable for individuals who prefer applied engineering, enjoy working with machines, and want to bridge the gap between engineering theory and real-world implementation.

title: Executive MBA
