description: Executive MBA is an advanced program designed for experienced professionals and managers to develop strategic leadership, business management, and decision-making skills. It covers areas such as finance, operations, marketing, and organizational leadership with a strong focus on real-world application, executive thinking, and cross-functional management. This field suits mid- to senior-level professionals aiming to advance into leadership roles, expand their business knowledge, and drive organizational growth.

title: Aviation Management
description: Aviation Management focuses on the business, operations, and administration of the aviation industry, including airlines, airports, and aviation services. Students study aviation operations, airport management, aviation law, safety regulations, logistics, and business strategy while developing leadership and decision-making skills. This field suits individuals interested in both aviation and business, who prefer management, coordination, and operational roles rather than technical piloting or engineering.

title: Gastronomy and Culinary Arts
description: This field focuses on the art and science of food preparation, culinary techniques, and gastronomy within cultural and professional contexts. Students learn cooking methods, kitchen management, food safety, nutrition basics, and culinary creativity while developing practical skills and understanding global cuisines. It is suitable for individuals interested in becoming professional chefs, working in restaurants or hospitality, and pursuing careers that combine creativity with food and dining experiences.

title: Interior Architecture and Environmental Design
description: This field focuses on designing interior spaces that are functional, aesthetic, and environmentally sustainable, integrating architectural principles with environmental considerations. Students learn spatial design, materials, lighting, building systems, and sustainable design practices while developing creative and technical skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in designing built environments, improving interior functionality, and creating spaces that balance aesthetics with environmental impact.

title: Management Information Systems
description: Management Information Systems focuses on using technology and data systems to support business operations and decision-making. Students learn information systems, database management, business analytics, IT strategy, and system design to bridge the gap between business needs and technological solutions. The field suits individuals who are analytical, tech-oriented, and interested in applying IT to improve organizational performance.

title: Woodwind, Brass, or Percussion Performance (BM)
description: Woodwind, Brass, or Percussion Performance focuses on developing advanced performance skills on orchestral and band instruments such as flute, clarinet, trumpet, trombone, or percussion. The program provides intensive training in instrumental technique, musical interpretation, sight-reading, and stage performance while building strong foundations in music theory, ear training, and music history. Students gain extensive experience through orchestras, wind ensembles, chamber groups, and solo recitals, while also learning collaborative musicianship and professional performance practices. This field is suitable for individuals who are dedicated to mastering a wind, brass, or percussion instrument and pursuing professional careers in performance, recording, or music education.

title: IT: Network Administration
description: This field focuses on the installation, configuration, and management of computer networks within organizations. Students learn networking fundamentals, system administration, cybersecurity basics, cloud services, and troubleshooting techniques, with an emphasis on hands-on technical skills. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy working with IT systems, solving technical issues, and maintaining reliable and secure network infrastructure.

title: Human Services Concentration
description: Human Services Concentration focuses on supporting individuals, families, and communities by addressing social, emotional, and practical needs through community programs and social support systems. Students learn about social services, community resources, case management, and social policies that affect vulnerable populations. The program develops skills in communication, problem-solving, and service coordination while emphasizing ethical practice and advocacy. This field is suitable for individuals who are compassionate, socially aware, and interested in working directly with people to improve well-being and access to community support services.

title: Clinical Mental Health Counseling
description: Clinical Mental Health Counseling focuses on preparing professionals to assess, diagnose, and treat emotional, behavioral, and psychological challenges in individuals, couples, and groups. The program combines studies in counseling theories, psychotherapy techniques, human development, and psychological assessment with practical training in ethical practice and clinical supervision. Students learn how to support clients dealing with issues such as anxiety, depression, trauma, relationship difficulties, and life transitions. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in mental health, empathetic communication, and helping people improve their psychological well-being through professional counseling.

title: Communication System & Network Engineering
description: Communication System & Network Engineering focuses on the design, implementation, and management of infrastructure that enables the high-speed exchange of information across global distances. Students acquire expertise in wireless communications, fiber optics, network security protocols, and satellite systems while mastering technologies like 5G/6G, SDN (Software-Defined Networking), and signal processing. This field is ideal for technical thinkers interested in the "backbone" of the digital age. The curriculum emphasizes the transition from physical signal transmission to complex network architectures, providing the engineering foundation to build reliable, scalable, and secure communication links for everything from mobile devices to critical government infrastructure.

title: Entrepreneurship Concentration
description: Entrepreneurship Concentration focuses on the process of creating, developing, and managing new business ventures. Students learn how to identify market opportunities, develop business models, secure funding, and launch innovative products or services. The curriculum typically includes startup strategy, business planning, innovation management, marketing, and financial decision-making. This field is suitable for individuals who are creative, proactive, and interested in building new businesses or leading innovation within existing organizations.

title: Entrepreneurship
description: Entrepreneurship focuses on creating, developing, and managing new business ventures, emphasizing innovation, opportunity recognition, and risk management. Students study business planning, finance, marketing, and strategy while developing practical skills in launching and scaling startups. This field suits individuals who are proactive, creative, and willing to take risks, with a strong interest in building businesses and driving innovation.

title: Individual Studies
description: This field allows students to design a customized program of study by combining courses from multiple disciplines based on their interests and career goals. Students develop interdisciplinary knowledge, critical thinking, and flexibility by integrating subjects such as humanities, sciences, or social sciences. It is suitable for individuals who have specific interests that do not fit into a single major and want a personalized academic pathway.

title: Doctor Of Nursing Practice Program
description: The Doctor of Nursing Practice (DNP) is a terminal practice-focused doctoral degree designed to prepare advanced practice nurses for clinical leadership, evidence-based practice, healthcare systems improvement, and policy implementation. The program emphasizes advanced clinical practice, healthcare quality and safety, leadership, informatics, population health, and translation of research into practice, equipping graduates to improve patient outcomes and lead change across healthcare systems.

title: Advanced Master of Divinity
description: The Advanced Master of Divinity is a graduate-level professional degree designed for advanced preparation in theological scholarship, pastoral leadership, ministry strategy, ethics, and applied theology. Building on foundational ministerial training, the program emphasizes advanced biblical interpretation, leadership formation, contextual ministry, and supervised practice, preparing graduates for senior ministry roles, chaplaincy leadership, or doctoral-level study.

title: Marketing
description: Marketing is a business discipline focused on understanding consumer behavior and creating strategies to promote products, services, and brands. The field integrates market research, branding, digital marketing, advertising, sales strategy, and data analytics to drive customer engagement and business growth across industries.

title: Music Education
description: Music Education focuses on preparing students to teach music in schools, community programs, and educational institutions. The program combines studies in music theory, ear training, music history, conducting, and performance with courses in pedagogy, curriculum design, and educational psychology. Students learn how to teach vocal and instrumental music, lead ensembles such as choirs or bands, and design engaging music learning experiences for different age groups. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about music and enjoy guiding others in developing musical skills and appreciation.

title: Master of Arts in English
description: The Master of Arts in English focuses on advanced study of literature, linguistics, rhetoric, and critical theory, emphasizing analytical reading, academic writing, and research skills. Programs often allow specialization in areas such as Literary Studies, Linguistics, Creative Writing, or Teaching English, preparing graduates for professional roles and doctoral study.

title: Global and Intercultural Leadership
description: Global and Intercultural Leadership is an interdisciplinary field that examines the complex dynamics of leading diverse groups within an increasingly interconnected world. The discipline integrates sociology, cultural anthropology, and organizational psychology to develop competencies in cross-cultural communication, conflict resolution, and global strategic management. It is designed for individuals who aim to navigate and influence multicultural environments, fostering inclusive practices and effective collaboration across national and social boundaries in corporate, governmental, or non-profit sectors.

title: Advanced Nuclear Waste Management
description: Advanced Nuclear Waste Management focuses on the technologies and strategies used to safely handle, treat, store, and dispose of radioactive waste generated from nuclear energy production and related industries. Students study nuclear engineering principles, radiation protection, environmental safety, waste treatment technologies, and long-term storage systems. The program emphasizes risk management, regulatory compliance, and sustainable solutions for reducing the environmental impact of nuclear waste. This field is suitable for individuals interested in energy systems, environmental protection, and engineering solutions for managing hazardous nuclear materials.

title: Family Nurse Practitioner
description: Family Nurse Practitioner (FNP) is an advanced practice specialization in nursing that prepares registered nurses to provide primary healthcare for patients across all age groups, from infants to older adults. The program focuses on advanced clinical assessment, diagnosis, disease management, pharmacology, and preventive care. Students learn how to evaluate patient conditions, develop treatment plans, prescribe medications in many healthcare systems, and promote long-term health within families and communities. This field is suitable for nurses who want to take on expanded clinical responsibilities and deliver comprehensive primary care in diverse healthcare settings.

title: Building Trades: Building Construction
description: This field focuses on the fundamental skills and knowledge required for building construction, including structural systems, construction methods, materials, and site operations. Students learn blueprint reading, project coordination, safety standards, and hands-on construction techniques. It is suitable for individuals who are interested in the construction industry, enjoy practical work, and want to develop skills for building and managing construction projects.

title: International Finance
description: This field focuses on financial management and investment activities in a global context, integrating finance principles with international markets and economic systems. Students learn international financial markets, foreign exchange, cross-border investments, risk management, and global financial strategy while developing analytical and quantitative skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in global finance, working with multinational corporations, financial institutions, and roles involving international capital flows and financial decision-making.

title: Data Science
description: Data Science focuses on extracting meaningful insights from large and complex datasets using statistical analysis, machine learning, and computational techniques. Students learn programming, data analysis, predictive modeling, and data visualization while working with tools used to process and interpret structured and unstructured data. The program combines knowledge from mathematics, statistics, and computer science to support data-driven decision making in organizations. This field is suitable for individuals who enjoy analytical thinking, working with data, and using technology to uncover patterns that help businesses and institutions make strategic decisions.

title: Early Childhood Education
description: Early Childhood Education focuses on the formal instruction and developmental support of children during their most formative years, typically from birth through age eight. Students acquire expertise in literacy development, mathematical foundations, social-emotional learning, and classroom assessment while mastering the art of creating structured yet creative learning environments. This field is ideal for patient, creative leaders who are passionate about shaping the cognitive and behavioral trajectories of young learners. The curriculum emphasizes the application of educational psychology to design age-appropriate curricula that meet diverse learning needs, providing the pedagogical tools to navigate state licensing requirements and national educational standards.

title: Health Information Technology
description: Health Information Technology focuses on managing and analyzing healthcare data and information systems to support clinical and administrative decision-making. The program covers topics such as health informatics, electronic health records, medical coding, data management, healthcare systems, and information security. Students learn how to organize, protect, and use health data to improve patient care, ensure regulatory compliance, and optimize healthcare operations. This field is suitable for individuals interested in healthcare and technology, particularly in working with data systems that support medical services and health organizations.

title: Sport Coaching and Fitness Concentration
description: Sport Coaching and Fitness Concentration focuses on training, coaching, and improving athletic performance and physical fitness. Students learn about exercise science, coaching techniques, sports training methods, and athlete development while also gaining knowledge in fitness programming, injury prevention, and sports leadership. The program prepares students to guide individuals or teams in achieving physical performance goals and maintaining healthy lifestyles. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about sports, fitness, and helping others improve their athletic abilities and overall physical well-being.

title: Industrial Engineering
description: Industrial Engineering focuses on optimizing complex systems, processes, and organizations by integrating engineering principles with management, data analysis, and human factors. Students learn operations research, process improvement, supply chain management, quality control, and systems design, along with analytical tools and technologies. The field suits individuals who enjoy problem-solving, efficiency improvement, working with both technical systems and business operations, and making data-driven decisions across industries.

title: Commercial Music (BS)
description: Commercial Music focuses on the creation, production, and business aspects of music for modern media and entertainment industries. Students study music theory, songwriting, audio production, music technology, performance, music marketing, and the music business. The program prepares individuals to produce and distribute music professionally across platforms such as streaming, film, television, and digital media. It is suitable for creative individuals who are passionate about music, technology, and the commercial side of the entertainment industry.

title: MBA
description: MBA focuses on developing comprehensive business knowledge and management skills across areas such as finance, marketing, operations, strategy, and leadership. Students learn how to analyze business problems, make strategic decisions, and manage organizations effectively while gaining practical, real-world insights. This field suits individuals aiming to build or advance careers in business, management, and leadership roles across a wide range of industries.

title: Management
description:
Management focuses on planning, organizing, leading, and controlling organizational resources to achieve strategic goals. The field develops leadership, decision-making, problem-solving, and operational management skills applicable across industries.
Students study organizational behavior, strategy, human resource management, operations, entrepreneurship, leadership, and corporate governance.
This is a broad business discipline suitable for students who want managerial roles rather than technical specialization.

title: Supply Chain Management
description: Supply Chain Management focuses on planning, coordinating, and optimizing the flow of goods, services, information, and finances from suppliers to customers. Students study logistics, procurement, inventory management, operations, and supply chain strategy while developing analytical and problem-solving skills to improve efficiency and reduce costs. This field suits individuals who are organized, detail-oriented, and interested in operations, global trade, and managing complex systems across industries.

title: Music Instrumental (BS)
description: Music Instrumental focuses on developing advanced performance skills on a primary musical instrument while building a strong foundation in music theory, ear training, music history, and ensemble performance. The program provides intensive training in instrumental technique, interpretation, sight-reading, and stage presentation across classical and contemporary repertoires. Students gain practical experience through orchestras, bands, chamber ensembles, and live performances while also studying music technology and collaborative musicianship. This field is suitable for individuals who are dedicated to mastering an instrument and pursuing professional careers in performance, recording, teaching, or the broader music industry.

title: Marriage and Family Counseling
description: Marriage and Family Counseling focuses on assessing and treating relationship dynamics, family systems, and interpersonal conflicts. The field applies psychological principles and therapeutic techniques to help couples and families improve communication, resolve conflict, and address emotional or behavioral challenges. It prepares professionals to work in clinical and community settings, supporting mental health and relational well-being.

title: Industrial and Organizational Psychology
description: Industrial and Organizational Psychology focuses on applying psychological principles to workplace environments to improve employee performance, well-being, and organizational effectiveness. Students study human behavior at work, talent management, leadership, motivation, organizational development, and data-driven decision-making. The field suits individuals who are analytical, people-oriented, and interested in optimizing workplace performance and organizational success.

title: Professional Communication
description: This major focuses on effective communication within organizational and professional environments. It develops skills in business communication, interpersonal communication, leadership messaging, digital communication, and stakeholder engagement. The field prepares professionals to manage internal and external communication, coordinate cross-functional collaboration, and support strategic organizational goals. It is suitable for individuals interested in corporate environments, leadership communication, and professional consulting roles.

title: English
description: English focuses on the study of literature, language, writing, and critical analysis of texts from different cultures and historical periods. Students develop strong skills in reading, analytical thinking, research, and written communication while studying literary works, rhetoric, composition, and linguistic structures. The program helps students understand how language shapes ideas, culture, and communication. This field is suitable for individuals who enjoy reading, writing, analyzing texts, and developing strong communication and critical thinking skills.

title: IT: Web Development
description: This field focuses on designing, building, and maintaining websites and web applications. Students learn programming languages such as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, along with backend development, databases, user experience, and web frameworks. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy coding, problem-solving, and creating digital products for online platforms.

title: Automotive Service Technician
description: This field focuses on the maintenance, diagnosis, and repair of vehicles, including engines, electrical systems, and mechanical components. Students gain practical skills in automotive systems, troubleshooting techniques, and the use of diagnostic tools, along with foundational knowledge of mechanics and engineering principles. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy hands-on work, problem-solving, and working with vehicles and machinery in technical environments.

title: Exercise Science and Wellness
description: Exercise Science and Wellness focuses on understanding how physical activity, fitness, and lifestyle habits affect human health and overall well-being. The program combines studies in exercise physiology, human anatomy, biomechanics, nutrition, and health promotion while teaching students how to design safe and effective fitness and wellness programs. Students learn how to improve physical performance, prevent injuries, and promote healthy lifestyles for individuals and communities. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in fitness, health improvement, and helping others achieve better physical and mental well-being.

title: Masonry
description: This field focuses on the construction and installation of structures using materials such as brick, stone, and concrete blocks. Students learn techniques in masonry work, blueprint reading, material handling, and structural fundamentals, along with safety standards and construction practices. It is suitable for individuals who prefer hands-on work, have attention to detail, and are interested in building durable structures within the construction industry.

title: International Marketing Management
description: International Marketing Management focuses on planning and executing marketing strategies across global markets, taking into account cultural differences, international consumer behavior, and global market dynamics. Students study international marketing strategy, branding, market research, digital marketing, and cross-cultural communication while developing skills to adapt products and campaigns for diverse markets. This field suits individuals who are interested in marketing, global business, and working in multicultural environments.

title: Project Management Concentration
description: Project Management Concentration focuses on planning, organizing, and overseeing projects to ensure they are completed on time, within scope, and within budget. Students learn how to manage resources, coordinate teams, assess risks, and monitor project performance using structured management frameworks. The program typically covers project planning, operations management, leadership, budgeting, and strategic decision-making. This field is suitable for individuals who are organized, detail-oriented, and interested in leading teams and managing complex projects across industries.

title: Court and Realtime Reporting
description: Court and Realtime Reporting is a professional field focused on creating accurate verbatim transcripts of legal proceedings, meetings, broadcasts, and live events. The discipline combines advanced stenography or voice-writing technology with legal knowledge, language precision, and real-time captioning skills. Professionals in this field support courts, attorneys, government agencies, and media organizations by ensuring official records and accessibility services.

title: Electrical and Electronics
description: Electrical and Electronics focuses on the study and application of electricity, electronics, and electromagnetic systems, covering areas such as circuit design, power systems, telecommunications, embedded systems, and control engineering. Students develop strong foundations in mathematics, physics, and programming while learning to design, analyze, and optimize electrical and electronic devices and systems. This field suits individuals interested in technology, problem-solving, innovation, and working with both hardware and software across industries such as energy, automation, and communications.

title: Human Resource Management
description: Human Resource Management focuses on managing an organization’s workforce and developing strategies that support employee performance and organizational success. Students learn how to recruit and retain talent, manage employee relations, design compensation and benefits programs, and ensure compliance with labor laws and workplace policies. The curriculum typically covers organizational behavior, leadership, talent development, and workforce planning. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in people management, organizational strategy, and creating productive and supportive workplace environments.

title: Business
description: Business focuses on the principles and practices involved in managing organizations, markets, and economic activities. The program provides knowledge in areas such as management, marketing, finance, accounting, entrepreneurship, and business strategy. Students learn how organizations operate, how to analyze markets and financial data, and how to make effective managerial decisions in competitive environments. This field is suitable for individuals interested in leadership, organizational management, and developing skills to operate or grow businesses in various industries.

title: STEM Mathematics
description: STEM Mathematics focuses on developing strong analytical and quantitative skills through the study of mathematical concepts used in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics fields. The program covers areas such as calculus, algebra, statistics, mathematical modeling, and problem-solving techniques while emphasizing practical applications in scientific and technological contexts. Students learn how to analyze complex data, develop mathematical models, and support innovation in technical and research environments. This field is suitable for individuals who enjoy logical reasoning, quantitative analysis, and applying mathematical methods to solve real-world problems across STEM industries.

title: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning
description: This field focuses on the design, installation, maintenance, and optimization of heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems in residential, commercial, and industrial buildings. Students learn thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, refrigeration, electrical systems, and energy efficiency, along with hands-on technical skills. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy practical problem-solving, working with mechanical systems, and ensuring indoor environmental comfort and system performance.

title: IT: Applications Software Development
description: This field focuses on designing, developing, and maintaining software applications for computers and mobile devices. Students learn programming languages, software development processes, data structures, databases, and system design, along with testing and debugging techniques. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy coding, logical thinking, and building software solutions for real-world problems.

title: Master of Fine Arts in Studio Art
description: The Master of Fine Arts in Studio Art is a terminal degree that develops advanced creative practice, critical thinking, and professional artistic skills. The program emphasizes studio-based work, conceptual development, critique, and exhibition, allowing students to specialize in areas such as painting, sculpture, photography, printmaking, ceramics, or mixed media, while preparing for professional artistic and academic careers.

title: String Performance (BM)
description: String Performance focuses on developing advanced performance skills on string instruments such as violin, viola, cello, or double bass. The program provides intensive training in instrumental technique, musical interpretation, sight-reading, and stage performance while building strong foundations in music theory, ear training, and music history. Students gain extensive experience through orchestras, chamber ensembles, and solo recitals while learning collaborative musicianship and professional performance practices. This field is suitable for individuals who are dedicated to mastering a string instrument and pursuing professional careers in orchestral performance, chamber music, recording, or music education.

title: Liberal Arts & Sciences: Adolescent Education - Teacher Education Transfer
description: This major focuses on preparing students to teach adolescent learners in middle and high school settings. Students study pedagogy, curriculum design, adolescent development, and subject-specific teaching methods. It is suitable for individuals interested in teaching, mentoring, and shaping the academic and personal growth of teenagers.

title: Nursing (RN To BSN)
description: Nursing (RN to BSN) is a bridge program designed for registered nurses who want to advance their education to the bachelor’s level. The program builds on existing clinical experience and focuses on areas such as leadership, evidence-based practice, community health, healthcare policy, and advanced patient care. Students enhance their critical thinking, management, and professional skills to take on broader responsibilities in healthcare settings. This field is suitable for registered nurses seeking career advancement, higher-level roles, and expanded opportunities in clinical and administrative positions.

title: Allied Health Concentration
description: Allied Health Concentration focuses on healthcare support professions that assist in the diagnosis, treatment, and rehabilitation of patients. Students learn foundational knowledge in human anatomy, physiology, healthcare systems, patient care practices, and medical terminology while developing practical skills used in clinical and healthcare support settings. The program prepares students to work alongside physicians, nurses, and other healthcare professionals to improve patient outcomes. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in healthcare careers but may prefer roles in clinical support, rehabilitation, or healthcare services rather than direct physician training.

title: Adult Gerontology Acute Care Post-Master Certification
description: The Adult-Gerontology Acute Care Post-Master’s Certificate is designed for licensed Advanced Practice Registered Nurses (APRNs) who already hold a master’s degree in nursing and seek specialization in acute and complex care for adolescents, adults, and older adults. The program emphasizes advanced clinical assessment, diagnosis, critical care management, invasive procedures, and interprofessional collaboration in high-acuity settings. Graduates are eligible to sit for AGACNP national certification (ANCC or AACN).

title: Agriculture: Animal and Plant Science
description: This major focuses on the scientific principles underlying the production and management of plants and animals. It integrates biology, chemistry, and genetics to improve crop yields, enhance soil fertility, and optimize animal health and nutrition. The discipline addresses global challenges such as sustainable food production, pest control, and livestock management, preparing professionals to apply scientific innovations to the traditional sectors of farming and resource conservation.

title: Master of Divinity in Community Chaplaincy
description: Master of Divinity in Community Chaplaincy prepares students to provide spiritual guidance, pastoral care, and emotional support within community settings. The program combines studies in theology, pastoral counseling, ethics, and religious leadership with training in community outreach, crisis response, and interfaith communication. Students learn how to support individuals and families facing social challenges, grief, trauma, or life transitions while working with diverse cultural and religious communities. This field is suitable for individuals committed to faith-based service who want to work beyond traditional religious institutions by providing spiritual care and guidance in community organizations and public service environments.

title: Communication and Media Studies
description: Communication and Media Studies focuses on how information, messages, and media content are created, distributed, and interpreted in modern society. Students study communication theories, media systems, digital media production, audience behavior, and the social impact of media. The program helps develop strong skills in critical thinking, storytelling, media analysis, and strategic communication across various platforms such as television, social media, journalism, and digital content. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in media, communication strategy, and understanding how information shapes public opinion and culture.

title: Radiologic Technology
description: This field focuses on the use of medical imaging technologies such as X-rays, CT scans, and MRI to diagnose and monitor medical conditions. Students learn anatomy, radiographic procedures, imaging equipment operation, radiation safety, and patient care. It is suitable for individuals interested in healthcare technology, who are detail-oriented, and prefer hands-on clinical work supporting diagnosis rather than direct treatment.

title: Health Information Management Concentration
description: Health Information Management Concentration focuses on the management, organization, and protection of healthcare data and medical records within healthcare systems. Students learn how to work with electronic health records, health data systems, medical coding, healthcare regulations, and information governance. The program combines healthcare knowledge with data management and information technology to ensure accurate, secure, and efficient use of patient information. This field is suitable for individuals who are detail-oriented, interested in healthcare systems, and comfortable working with data, technology, and regulatory standards in medical environments.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering
description: Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering focuses on building intelligent systems and scalable data infrastructures that enable data collection, processing, analysis, and automation. Students learn machine learning, data engineering, databases, distributed systems, and programming while developing the ability to design end-to-end data pipelines and AI-driven solutions. This field suits individuals who are analytical, interested in both data and technology, and enjoy working with large-scale systems, algorithms, and real-world problem-solving.

title: International Management
description: This field focuses on managing business operations across international markets, combining management principles with global economics, cross-cultural communication, and international strategy. Students learn areas such as global business strategy, international finance, trade, marketing, and organizational management in multicultural environments. It is suitable for individuals interested in working in multinational companies, global markets, and roles that require adaptability, strategic thinking, and cross-border coordination.

title: Finance Concentration
description: Finance Concentration focuses on the management of money, investments, and financial decision-making within organizations and markets. Students learn how financial systems operate while developing skills in financial analysis, investment strategies, risk management, corporate finance, and financial planning. The program helps students understand how businesses raise capital, manage assets, and evaluate financial opportunities. This field is suitable for individuals who are analytical, detail-oriented, and interested in financial markets, corporate strategy, and investment management.

title: Digital Media and Animation
description: This field focuses on creating digital visual content, including animation, motion graphics, and multimedia design. Students learn storytelling, visual design, animation techniques, 2D/3D software, and digital production processes. It is suitable for individuals who are creative, enjoy visual storytelling, and want to work in media, entertainment, or digital content industries.

title: Agribusiness Management
description: This major focuses on managing agricultural enterprises and the business side of food production. Students study farm management, agricultural finance, supply chains, marketing, and agribusiness operations. It is suitable for individuals interested in combining business strategy with agriculture, particularly those aiming to run farms, agricultural companies, or food production businesses.

title: Agricultural Business
description: Agricultural Business is a field focused on the management, marketing, finance, and operations of agricultural enterprises and food production systems. It integrates agricultural knowledge with business principles such as supply chain management, agribusiness finance, trade, and sustainable resource management to improve productivity and profitability within the agriculture and food industries.

title: Nursing (BSN)
description: Nursing (BSN) is an undergraduate program that prepares students to provide professional patient care in healthcare settings. The curriculum combines medical science with clinical training, covering subjects such as human anatomy, physiology, pharmacology, patient assessment, and healthcare ethics. Students develop practical skills in clinical practice while learning how to support patients, coordinate with healthcare teams, and promote health and disease prevention. This field is well suited for individuals who are compassionate, responsible, detail-oriented, and interested in working directly with patients in hospitals, clinics, and community health environments.

title: Undeclared Major
description: This field represents a flexible academic path where students explore a range of disciplines before selecting a specific major. Students take courses across areas such as humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences, developing broad knowledge, critical thinking, and academic skills. It is suitable for individuals who are undecided about their career direction and want time to discover their interests before committing to a specialized field.

title: Construction Engineering Technology
description: This field focuses on the practical application of engineering principles in construction projects, including planning, materials, methods, and site operations. Students learn structural basics, construction technology, cost estimation, scheduling, and quality control, with an emphasis on hands-on and technical skills. It is suitable for individuals who are interested in construction and engineering, enjoy working on project sites, and want to bridge the gap between design and execution.

title: Human Services Management
description: This major focuses on managing human services organizations and programs that support communities, families, and individuals. Students learn about social policy, program administration, leadership, budgeting, and organizational management in social service settings. It is suitable for individuals interested in combining management skills with social impact, particularly those aiming to lead non-profits, community programs, or social service agencies.

title: Health Sciences
description: Health Sciences is a multidisciplinary field that focuses on understanding human health, disease prevention, and the functioning of healthcare systems. Students study subjects such as human biology, public health, healthcare policy, epidemiology, and health promotion while developing knowledge about how healthcare services are delivered and managed. The program often serves as preparation for various health-related careers or advanced professional programs. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in improving health outcomes, working in healthcare environments, or pursuing further study in medical, clinical, or public health professions.

title: Culinary Arts: Baking, Production & Management
description: This field focuses on baking and pastry production combined with kitchen and business management. Students learn baking techniques, ingredient functions, food safety, production planning, and kitchen operations, along with cost control and team management. It is suitable for individuals who are passionate about baking, enjoy hands-on creative work, and want to manage or operate bakery or food production businesses.

title: Veterinary Technology
description: Veterinary Technology focuses on the advanced medical and clinical support required for the healthcare of domestic, exotic, and farm animals. Students master the complexities of veterinary pharmacology, anesthesia, surgical nursing, and diagnostic imaging while developing a deep understanding of animal anatomy and physiology. The curriculum integrates high-level laboratory sciences with the ethical and practical application of animal care in clinical settings. This field is ideal for compassionate, detail-oriented individuals who aim to work alongside veterinarians to provide life-saving treatments, manage animal hospitals, and contribute to the overall health and welfare of the animal kingdom through scientific innovation.

title: Engineering
description:
Engineering applies mathematics, science, and technology to design, build, and optimize systems, structures, machines, and processes that solve real-world problems.
This is a broad umbrella major that includes Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Industrial Engineering, and more.
Students study physics, advanced mathematics, design principles, systems thinking, and specialized technical subjects depending on their concentration.
Engineering is ideal for students who enjoy problem-solving, technical design, innovation, and applied science.

title: Financial Planning
description: Financial Planning is a business discipline focused on helping individuals and organizations manage finances, investments, risk, taxation, retirement, and long-term wealth strategies. The field integrates financial analysis, portfolio management, insurance planning, estate planning, and advisory services to support sustainable financial growth and informed decision-making. It combines technical financial knowledge with client advisory and strategic planning skills.

title: Master Of Education In Educational Administration
description: The Master of Education in Educational Administration prepares educators for leadership and administrative roles within educational institutions. The program emphasizes school leadership, educational policy, curriculum planning, human resource management, budgeting, data-driven decision making, and ethical administration, equipping graduates to lead schools, districts, and educational organizations effectively. Many programs also align with principal or administrator licensure requirements, depending on jurisdiction.

title: Master of Divinity in Military Chaplaincy
description: Master of Divinity in Military Chaplaincy prepares students to serve as chaplains providing spiritual, emotional, and pastoral support within military environments. The program combines advanced studies in theology, pastoral care, ethics, and religious leadership with specialized training in counseling, crisis response, and ministry in military contexts. Students learn how to support service members and their families, address moral and spiritual challenges, and provide guidance during deployments, conflicts, and transitions. This field is suitable for individuals called to religious leadership who want to serve in the armed forces by offering spiritual care and counseling in demanding and diverse environments.

title: Heavy Equipment Operations
description: This field focuses on operating and managing heavy machinery used in construction, infrastructure, and industrial projects. Students learn how to handle equipment such as excavators, bulldozers, loaders, and cranes, along with safety procedures, site operations, and basic mechanical knowledge. It is suitable for individuals who prefer hands-on work, are interested in large-scale construction activities, and want to work in dynamic outdoor or industrial environments.

title: Early Intervention Concentration
description: Early Intervention Concentration focuses on supporting infants and young children who experience developmental delays or disabilities. Students learn how to assess developmental needs, design intervention strategies, and work collaboratively with families, educators, and healthcare professionals to support early learning and development. The curriculum typically includes child development, special education principles, developmental assessment, and family-centered intervention practices. This field is suitable for individuals who are patient, compassionate, and interested in helping young children achieve developmental milestones and succeed in early learning environments.

title: Agricultural Automation and Robotics
description: Agricultural Automation and Robotics is an interdisciplinary field that integrates mechanical engineering, software development, and agricultural science to optimize food production. It focuses on the design and deployment of autonomous vehicles, drones, and robotic systems for tasks such as precision planting, automated harvesting, and real-time crop monitoring. By leveraging AI and sensor technology, this discipline aims to increase farming efficiency, reduce labor dependency, and promote environmental sustainability through precise resource management.

title: Master of Divinity in Chaplain Ministries
description: This major focuses on the study of religious thought, ethical systems, and philosophical traditions, including theological education such as Divinity and Chaplain Ministries. Students explore moral philosophy, comparative religion, spiritual care, leadership, and applied ethics while developing critical thinking and reflective reasoning skills. The program is suitable for students interested in faith-based leadership, ethical guidance, counseling, academic research, and community service roles.

title: Pastoral Counseling
description: Pastoral Counseling is an integrative field that combines psychological principles and therapeutic techniques with spiritual and theological insights to promote holistic healing. The discipline focuses on addressing mental health issues, relationship conflicts, and emotional distress within a framework that respects and utilizes an individual's faith or spiritual journey.

title: Fine Arts in Graphic Design
description: Fine Arts in Graphic Design focuses on the intersection of visual communication, artistic expression, and technology. This major emphasizes the development of a strong conceptual foundation combined with technical proficiency in typography, layout, branding, and digital media. Students explore the principles of design as a form of visual problem-solving, learning to create compelling narratives for print, web, and interactive platforms. It is ideal for individuals who want to master the aesthetics of visual language while understanding the psychological and social impact of design in a commercial and cultural context.

title: Liberal Arts
description: Liberal Arts focuses on a broad, interdisciplinary education that develops critical thinking, communication, and analytical skills across multiple fields of study. Students typically explore subjects in the humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences while learning how to evaluate ideas, interpret information, and communicate effectively. The program emphasizes intellectual flexibility and a well-rounded academic foundation rather than specialization in a single technical field. This field is suitable for individuals who enjoy exploring diverse subjects and developing versatile skills that can be applied across many professional areas.

title: Computer Engineering Technology
description: This field focuses on the practical application of computer engineering principles to design, build, and maintain computer systems and embedded hardware. Students learn programming, digital systems, microcontrollers, networking, and hardware-software integration, with an emphasis on hands-on technical skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in both hardware and software, and who want to work with computer systems, electronics, and modern technology solutions.

title: Early Childhood Education and Early Intervention
description: Early Childhood Education and Early Intervention focuses on the development, education, and support of young children, typically from birth through early elementary years. Students learn about child development, early learning strategies, classroom management, and educational assessment while also studying methods to support children with developmental delays or special needs. The program prepares students to create inclusive learning environments and collaborate with families and specialists to support children’s cognitive, social, and emotional development. This field is suitable for individuals who are patient, nurturing, and interested in supporting early childhood learning and development.

title: Motorsports Technology
description: This field focuses on the design, performance, and maintenance of high-speed vehicles used in racing and motorsports. Students learn about vehicle dynamics, engine performance, aerodynamics, data analysis, and advanced mechanical systems, often combining engineering theory with hands-on experience. It is suitable for individuals passionate about automotive performance, racing environments, and applying engineering principles to optimize speed, safety, and efficiency.

title: Biblical Interpretation
description: This major focuses on the study and interpretation of biblical texts, examining historical context, literary structure, theological themes, and hermeneutical methods. Students develop skills in critical analysis, textual interpretation, ethical reasoning, and religious scholarship. The program is suitable for students interested in theology, religious education, ministry leadership, and academic research within faith-based or scholarly settings.

title: Industrial Design
description: This field focuses on designing and developing products that are functional, aesthetic, and user-centered, combining creativity with technical and manufacturing knowledge. Students learn product design, materials, ergonomics, prototyping, and production processes while developing problem-solving and design thinking skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in creating physical products, improving user experience, and working at the intersection of design, technology, and innovation.

title: Liberal Arts & Sciences: Math & Science
description: This interdisciplinary field combines mathematical reasoning with scientific exploration of the natural world. Students develop strong quantitative, analytical, and problem-solving skills while studying core areas such as mathematics, physics, chemistry, and biology. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy logical thinking, working with data, and understanding how natural systems operate, and who seek flexible pathways into science, technology, research, or advanced study.

title: Applied Psychology
description: This major focuses on applying psychological principles to real-world problems in areas such as mental health, organizational behavior, education, and human development. Students learn about behavioral assessment, counseling techniques, research methods, and intervention strategies. It is suitable for individuals interested in understanding human behavior and using that knowledge to improve individual and community well-being.

title: Nursing Dual Degree
description: This field focuses on preparing students for professional nursing practice through a combination of theoretical knowledge and clinical training. Students learn patient care, medical procedures, anatomy, pharmacology, and healthcare ethics, often with an extended or dual-degree structure that may include additional academic or specialization components. It is suitable for individuals who are compassionate, detail-oriented, and interested in providing direct patient care in healthcare settings.

title: Luxury Management
description: Luxury Management focuses on managing high-end brands, products, and services across industries such as fashion, hospitality, retail, and lifestyle. Students study brand management, consumer behavior, marketing strategy, retail operations, and customer experience with an emphasis on exclusivity, storytelling, and premium positioning. This field suits individuals who are interested in business, branding, and the luxury market, with a strong sense of aesthetics, attention to detail, and an understanding of high-end consumer expectations.

title: Global Business and Management
description: Global Business and Management focuses on managing business activities across international markets, combining core management principles with global strategy, cross-cultural communication, and international operations. Students study areas such as international business, marketing, finance, and organizational management while developing the ability to operate in diverse cultural and economic environments. This field suits individuals who are interested in global markets, enjoy working across cultures, and aim to build careers in multinational organizations or international business settings.

title: Welding Technology
description: This field focuses on the techniques and technologies used to join metals and materials in construction, manufacturing, and industrial settings. Students learn welding processes such as MIG, TIG, and arc welding, along with blueprint reading, metallurgy basics, and safety standards. It is suitable for individuals who prefer hands-on work, have good precision and coordination, and are interested in working with machinery and metal structures.

title: American Sign Language
description: American Sign Language focuses on learning the language, grammar, and cultural context of sign language used by Deaf and hard-of-hearing communities in the United States and other regions. The program develops skills in visual communication, sign language interpretation, and cross-cultural understanding while covering linguistics, Deaf culture, and communication strategies. Students learn how to communicate effectively using signs, facial expressions, and body language while understanding the social and cultural aspects of the Deaf community. This field is suitable for individuals interested in language, accessibility, and supporting communication between Deaf and hearing communities.

title: Professional Flight
description: Professional Flight focuses on training students to become skilled pilots, combining aviation theory with extensive practical flight training. Students study aerodynamics, navigation, meteorology, aviation regulations, and aircraft systems while developing hands-on flying skills and safety procedures. This field suits individuals who are disciplined, detail-oriented, and passionate about aviation, with strong decision-making abilities and the ability to perform under pressure.

title: Game Development Concentration
description: Game Development Concentration focuses on the design and development of digital games across platforms such as computers, consoles, and mobile devices. Students learn how to build interactive game systems using programming, game engines, graphics, and software development tools. The curriculum typically covers game programming, computer graphics, game design principles, artificial intelligence for games, and user experience. This field is suitable for individuals who enjoy coding, problem-solving, and creating interactive digital experiences within the gaming and entertainment industry.

title: Applied Artificial Intelligence Concentration
description: Applied Artificial Intelligence Concentration focuses on the practical use of artificial intelligence technologies to solve real-world problems across industries. Students learn how to design, develop, and apply AI systems using techniques such as machine learning, data analysis, natural language processing, and intelligent automation. The program combines computer science fundamentals with hands-on experience in building AI-driven applications. This field is suitable for individuals who are analytical, interested in technology and data, and motivated to create intelligent systems that improve decision-making and efficiency.

title: Technology Management
description: Technology Management is a specialized field that bridges the gap between business management and engineering/technology. It focuses on the strategic planning, development, and implementation of technological capabilities to shape and accomplish the strategic and operational objectives of an organization. The discipline covers innovation management, R&D strategy, technology life cycle assessment, and digital transformation, preparing professionals to lead in tech-driven environments.

title: Master of Divinity in Healthcare Chaplaincy
description: Master of Divinity in Healthcare Chaplaincy prepares students to provide spiritual care and emotional support within healthcare environments such as hospitals, clinics, and hospice centers. The program combines studies in theology, pastoral counseling, ethics, and spiritual care with specialized training in healthcare ministry, crisis support, and interfaith communication. Students learn how to assist patients, families, and medical staff in coping with illness, grief, ethical dilemmas, and end-of-life situations while respecting diverse religious and cultural backgrounds. This field is suitable for individuals called to religious service who want to work in healthcare settings providing compassionate spiritual guidance and pastoral care.

title: Public Relations Concentration
description: Public Relations Concentration focuses on managing communication between organizations and their audiences to build and maintain a positive public image. Students learn how to develop communication strategies, manage media relations, create persuasive messaging, and handle public communication during both routine operations and crises. The curriculum typically covers strategic communication, media writing, branding, digital communication, and reputation management. This field is suitable for individuals who are strong communicators, strategic thinkers, and interested in shaping how organizations interact with the public and media.

title: Biblical Studies
description: Biblical Studies focuses on the academic study of the Bible, examining its historical context, literary structure, original languages, theology, and cultural impact. Students develop skills in textual analysis, critical thinking, historical research, and ethical reasoning while exploring ancient civilizations and religious traditions. The major is suitable for students interested in religion, history, philosophy, education, and research-oriented careers.

title: Early Childhood Education Director Concentration
description: Early Childhood Education Director Concentration focuses on leadership and management within early childhood education settings. Students learn how to oversee preschool or childcare programs while developing skills in educational leadership, curriculum planning, staff supervision, budgeting, and regulatory compliance. The program combines knowledge of child development and early learning with administrative and organizational management. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in leading early childhood programs and ensuring high-quality educational environments for young children.

title: Liberal Arts & Sciences: Social Science
description: Liberal Arts & Sciences: Social Science focuses on the systematic study of human society, social relationships, and individual behavior within a global context. Students master the complexities of psychological theories, sociological research methods, and political systems while developing high-level proficiency in data analysis and critical inquiry. The curriculum integrates the diverse perspectives of history, economics, and geography to analyze complex social challenges and institutional structures. This field is ideal for analytical thinkers and socially-conscious individuals who aim to build a strong academic bridge to bachelor’s programs in law, social work, public administration, or criminal justice, emphasizing the power of evidence-based reasoning and societal impact.

title: Juris Doctor (JD)
description: Juris Doctor (JD) focuses on preparing students for professional legal practice and the interpretation and application of law. The program provides comprehensive training in legal principles, including constitutional law, contracts, criminal law, civil procedure, property law, and legal research and writing. Students develop critical thinking, argumentation, and analytical skills while learning how to interpret legal systems, represent clients, and address complex legal issues in various sectors. This field is suitable for individuals interested in justice, public policy, and legal advocacy who want to pursue careers in legal practice, government, or corporate law.

title: Digital Marketing
description: Digital Marketing is a dynamic field that leverages the internet and online based technologies to reach consumers. The curriculum covers key disciplines such as Search Engine Optimization (SEO), Pay-Per-Click (PPC) advertising, content marketing, and social media strategy. Students learn to use analytics tools to track campaign performance, understand consumer behavior in the digital space, and create engaging content that drives brand awareness and sales.

title: Clinical Psychology
description:
Clinical Psychology focuses on the assessment, diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of mental health disorders and emotional difficulties.
The field combines psychological theory, research methods, psychotherapy techniques, and psychological testing to support individuals across the lifespan.
Students study abnormal psychology, psychopathology, psychological assessment, psychotherapy approaches (CBT, psychodynamic, humanistic), neuroscience, ethics, and research methods.

title: Marketing Management
description: Marketing Management focuses on planning, executing, and analyzing marketing strategies to promote products, services, and brands in competitive markets. Students study consumer behavior, market research, branding, digital marketing, and strategic management while developing skills in communication, data analysis, and decision-making. This field suits individuals who are creative yet analytical, interested in understanding customers, and motivated to drive business growth through effective marketing strategies.

title: Management and Optimization of Supply Chains and Transport
description: Management and Optimization of Supply Chains and Transport focuses on designing, managing, and improving the flow of goods, information, and resources across supply chain networks. Students learn about logistics operations, transportation systems, supply chain analytics, inventory management, and optimization strategies that improve efficiency and reduce operational costs. The program combines management principles with analytical and technological tools used to coordinate global supply chains. This field is suitable for individuals who are interested in logistics systems, transportation networks, and improving operational performance in global trade and distribution.

title: Master of Arts in Psychology, Mental Health Track
description: A Master of Arts (MA) in Psychology with a Mental Health track provides advanced training in psychological theories, diagnosis, and therapeutic techniques to prepare students for careers as counselors, therapists, or mental health service providers. These programs often integrate social, developmental, and neuroscientific approaches to mental health, focusing on practical skills for treating clients.

title: Music Education – Instrumental (BM)
description: Music Education – Instrumental prepares students to teach instrumental music in schools and community programs while developing strong skills in music performance and pedagogy. The program combines studies in instrumental technique, conducting, ensemble leadership, music theory, ear training, and music history with coursework in educational psychology and teaching methods. Students learn how to instruct individuals and ensembles, manage band or orchestra programs, and design music curricula for different age groups. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about instrumental music and want to build careers in music teaching, ensemble direction, and arts education.

title: Social Work
description: Social Work focuses on improving the well-being of individuals, families, and communities by addressing social inequality, access to resources, and systemic challenges. The field combines social policy, community intervention, and support services to assist vulnerable populations and promote social justice.

title: Cybersecurity Concentration
description: Cybersecurity Concentration focuses on protecting computer systems, networks, and digital information from cyber threats and unauthorized access. Students learn how to identify security vulnerabilities, implement security protocols, and respond to cyber incidents using technical and analytical approaches. The curriculum typically includes network security, cryptography, ethical hacking, digital forensics, and risk management. This field is suitable for individuals who are analytical, detail-oriented, and interested in safeguarding digital infrastructure and information in an increasingly connected world.

title: Software Engineering Concentration
description: Software Engineering Concentration focuses on the design, development, testing, and maintenance of software systems. Students learn how to build reliable and scalable software using programming languages, algorithms, and modern development frameworks. The curriculum typically covers software architecture, database systems, system design, debugging, quality assurance, and collaborative development practices. This field is suitable for individuals who enjoy problem-solving, logical thinking, and building digital solutions used in applications, platforms, and large-scale technology systems.

title: Business Administration
description: Business Administration is a comprehensive field of study that focuses on the fundamental principles of managing and operating an organization. This major integrates various core disciplines, including accounting, finance, marketing, human resources, and operations management, to develop a holistic understanding of how businesses function in a global marketplace. Students learn to apply analytical and strategic thinking to solve organizational challenges, optimize resources, and lead teams effectively. It is designed for individuals who aspire to leadership roles, fostering the skills necessary to drive innovation, manage risk, and ensure sustainable growth in diverse corporate and entrepreneurial environments.

title: Worship Leadership (BM)
description:
Worship Leadership focuses on preparing students to lead music and coordinate worship experiences in religious and faith-based settings. The program combines training in vocal or instrumental performance, worship music styles, conducting, music theory, and arranging with studies in theology, ministry leadership, and worship planning. Students learn how to organize worship services, direct choirs or worship bands, and manage music ministries while developing leadership, communication, and musical collaboration skills. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about music, leadership, and spiritual service, and who want to guide worship through music in churches and religious organizations.

The program combines training in vocal or instrumental performance, worship music styles, conducting, music theory, and arranging with studies in theology, ministry leadership, and worship planning. Students learn how to organize worship services, direct choirs or worship bands, and manage music ministries while developing leadership, communication, and musical collaboration skills. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about music, leadership, and spiritual service, and who want to guide worship through music in churches and religious organizations.

title: Healthcare Management
description: This field focuses on managing healthcare organizations, services, and operations to ensure efficient delivery of medical care. Students learn healthcare systems, administration, finance, policy, and leadership, along with organizational and strategic management skills. It is suitable for individuals interested in the healthcare sector who prefer management, coordination, and decision-making roles rather than direct clinical practice.

title: Osteopathic Medicine
description: Osteopathic Medicine prepares students to become physicians trained in a holistic approach to healthcare, emphasizing the interrelationship between structure and function of the body. Students study biomedical sciences, clinical medicine, pathology, pharmacology, and patient care, along with specialized training in osteopathic manipulative treatment (OMT). The program is rigorous and clinically intensive, designed for students committed to becoming licensed physicians who value comprehensive, patient-centered care.

title: Technical Studies
description: Technical Studies focuses on developing practical technical knowledge and applied problem-solving skills across various technology and industry-related fields. The program typically combines foundational studies in mathematics, science, and technology with hands-on training in technical systems, equipment operation, and applied engineering concepts. Students learn how to apply technical knowledge to real-world workplace environments, support technical operations, and adapt to different industrial or technological roles. This field is suitable for individuals interested in applied technical work and pursuing careers in technical support, industrial operations, and technology-related environments.

title: Vocal Performance (BM)
description: Vocal Performance focuses on developing advanced singing technique and artistic expression for professional stage and recording careers. The program provides intensive training in vocal technique, breath control, diction, interpretation, and stage performance across a range of musical styles such as classical, opera, and contemporary repertoire. Students also study music theory, ear training, language for singers, and music history while gaining extensive performance experience through recitals, operas, and ensembles. This field is suitable for individuals who are passionate about singing and want to pursue professional careers in vocal performance, recording, teaching, or the broader performing arts industry.

title: Counseling Psychology
description: Counseling Psychology focuses on understanding human behavior, emotional well-being, and psychological development while helping individuals cope with personal, social, and mental health challenges. Students study psychological theories, counseling techniques, human development, and therapeutic communication while learning how to support individuals in managing stress, relationships, and life transitions. The program prepares graduates to work in counseling, mental health support, and behavioral services. This field is suitable for individuals who are empathetic, interested in human behavior, and motivated to help others improve their mental and emotional well-being.

title: Game & Interactive Design
description: This field focuses on designing and developing interactive digital experiences, including video games and interactive media. Students learn game design principles, storytelling, user experience, programming basics, and 2D/3D asset creation, combining creativity with technical skills. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy both design and technology, are interested in gaming or interactive media, and want to create engaging digital products.

title: International Relations and Globalization
description: International Relations and Globalization examines the interactions between countries, international organizations, and global systems, focusing on politics, economics, diplomacy, and cross-border issues such as trade, security, and cultural exchange. Students study international policy, global governance, political theory, and the impact of globalization on societies and economies while developing analytical, research, and communication skills. This field suits individuals interested in world affairs, geopolitics, international cooperation, and understanding complex global challenges.

title: Advanced Standing Master of Social Work
description: The Advanced Standing Master of Social Work is an accelerated graduate program designed for students who already hold a Bachelor of Social Work (BSW) from an accredited institution. The program builds on foundational social work competencies and focuses on advanced clinical practice, leadership, policy analysis, and evidence-based interventions, enabling graduates to enter professional practice more quickly while meeting licensure requirements.

title: Computer Information Systems
description: This field focuses on the use of information technology to support business operations and decision-making. Students learn database management, information systems, networking, cybersecurity basics, and business processes, along with skills in analysis and system implementation. It is suitable for individuals who are interested in both technology and business, and want to work at the intersection of IT systems and organizational strategy.

title: Surveying & Geomatics Engineering Technology
description: This field focuses on measuring, mapping, and analyzing land, infrastructure, and geographic data for construction, planning, and environmental projects. Students learn surveying techniques, geospatial technologies, GPS systems, mapping software, and data analysis, combining fieldwork with technical skills. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy working both outdoors and with technology, and are interested in land development, infrastructure, and spatial data.

title: Accounting
description:
Accounting is a business discipline focused on recording, analyzing, and reporting financial information to support decision-making and ensure regulatory compliance.
At the associate level, students learn foundational bookkeeping and financial record management. The bachelor’s level develops comprehensive knowledge in financial reporting, auditing, taxation, and managerial accounting. The master’s level advances expertise in specialized areas such as forensic accounting, international standards, analytics, and strategic financial management.

title: Business Management
description: Business Management focuses on planning, organizing, and leading business operations across functions such as finance, marketing, human resources, and operations. Students develop skills in strategy, decision-making, leadership, and organizational management while gaining a broad understanding of how businesses operate. This field suits individuals who are interested in leadership roles, problem-solving, and managing teams or organizations across various industries.

title: Autobody Repair
description: This field focuses on repairing and restoring vehicle bodies after damage, including collision repair, frame alignment, surface preparation, and refinishing. Students learn techniques in metalwork, painting, and the use of specialized tools and materials, along with an understanding of vehicle structure and safety standards. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy hands-on work, attention to detail, and transforming damaged vehicles back to functional and aesthetic condition.

title: Cnc Manufacturing and Machining
description: This field focuses on precision manufacturing using computer numerical control (CNC) machines to produce metal and mechanical components. Students learn machining processes, CAD/CAM software, blueprint reading, materials, and quality control, along with hands-on operation of CNC equipment. It is suitable for individuals who enjoy working with machines, precision work, and combining technical skills with modern manufacturing technology.

title: Strategic Communication
